maml:description.maml:para: Copies external applications between organizations.

The Copy-PmExternalApplication cmdlet copies Platform Management external applications from source organizations to destination organizations. This cmdlet creates copies of external application configurations, including their authentication settings, permissions, and group associations, enabling external application management across multiple UiPath organizations.

The cmdlet supports copying external applications to multiple destination organizations simultaneously. External applications can be identified by their Name parameter, and the cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for copying multiple applications efficiently.

If the groups to which the applications belong do not exist in the destination organization, they are automatically created during the copy operation, ensuring complete application configuration transfer.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which external applications to copy and the -Destination parameter to specify the target organizations. The -Path parameter enables working with multiple source organizations when not operating from within a specific organization context.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies groups between organizations.

The Copy-PmGroup cmdlet copies Platform Management groups from source organizations to destination organizations. This cmdlet creates copies of group configurations, including their member associations and permissions, enabling group management across multiple UiPath organizations.

The cmdlet supports copying groups to multiple destination organizations simultaneously. Groups can be identified by their GroupName parameter, and the cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for copying multiple groups efficiently.

This cmdlet automatically adds entities such as local users, robot accounts, external applications, directory users, and directory groups with the same name to the copied group. However, if such entities with the same names do not exist in the destination organization, they will not be created automatically - only existing entities with matching names will be added to the copied group.

Use the -GroupName parameter to specify which groups to copy and the -Destination parameter to specify the target organizations. The -Path parameter enables working with multiple source organizations when not operating from within a specific organization context.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies the source drive name (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:), and -Destination specifies the target organization drives where groups should be copied.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/Group
OAuth required scopes: PM.Group
Required permissions:
Specifies the source organization drive. If not specified, the current organization will be used as the source.
Specifies the GroupName of the groups to be copied. If not specified, all groups will be copied.
Specifies the destination organization drives where groups should be copied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the destination organization drives where groups should be copied.
Specifies the GroupName of the groups to be copied. If not specified, all groups will be copied.
Specifies the source organization drive. If not specified, the current organization will be used as the source.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:) for source and destination organizations.

Groups contain member associations and permissions. When copying groups, entities with the same names (local users, robot accounts, external applications, directory users, and directory groups) are automatically added to the copied group if they exist in the destination organization. Entities that do not exist in the destination will not be created automatically. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies robot accounts between organizations.

The Copy-PmRobotAccount cmdlet copies Platform Management robot accounts from source organizations to destination organizations. This cmdlet creates copies of robot account configurations, including their authentication settings, permissions, and group associations, enabling robot account management across multiple UiPath organizations.

The cmdlet supports copying robot accounts to multiple destination organizations simultaneously. Robot accounts can be identified by their Name parameter, and the cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for copying multiple robot accounts efficiently.

If the groups to which the robot accounts belong do not exist in the destination organization, they are automatically created during the copy operation, ensuring complete account configuration transfer.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which robot accounts to copy and the -Destination parameter to specify the target organizations. The -Path parameter enables working with multiple source organizations when not operating from within a specific organization context.

This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies the source drive name (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:), and -Destination specifies the target organization drives where robot accounts should be copied.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/RobotAccount
OAuth required scopes: PM.RobotAccount
Required permissions:
Specifies the source organization drive. If not specified, the current organization will be used as the source.
Specifies the Name of the robot accounts to be copied.
Specifies the destination organization drives where robot accounts should be copied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the destination organization drives where robot accounts should be copied.
Specifies the Name of the robot accounts to be copied.
Specifies the source organization drive. If not specified, the current organization will be used as the source.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:) for source and destination organizations.

Robot accounts contain authentication settings, permissions, and group associations. If associated groups do not exist in the destination organization, they are automatically created. When copying across environments, verify that the robot account configurations are appropriate for the destination environment. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies organizational users between organizations.

The Copy-PmUser cmdlet copies Platform Management users from source organizations to destination organizations. This cmdlet creates copies of user configurations, including their permissions, settings, and group associations, enabling user management across multiple UiPath organizations.

The cmdlet supports copying users to multiple destination organizations simultaneously. Users can be identified by their Email parameter (which has an alias of UserName), and the cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for copying multiple users efficiently.
If the groups to which the users belong do not exist in the destination organization, they are automatically created during the copy operation, ensuring complete user configuration transfer.

Use the -Email parameter to specify which users to copy and the -Destination parameter to specify the target environments. The -Path parameter enables working with multiple source organizations when not operating from within a specific organization context.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies the source drive name (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:), and -Destination specifies the target organization drives where users should be copied.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/User/BulkCreate
OAuth required scopes: PM.User
Required permissions:
Specifies the source organization drive. If not specified, the current organization will be used as the source.
Specifies the Email addresses of the users to be copied. This parameter has an alias of UserName for compatibility.
Specifies the destination organization drives where users should be copied.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables API triggers in specified folders.

The Disable-OrchApiTrigger cmdlet disables API triggers in specified folders within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet allows you to temporarily suspend API trigger operations without deleting the trigger configurations, making it useful for maintenance scenarios or when troubleshooting automation workflows.

API triggers enable external systems to initiate UiPath processes through HTTP API calls. Disabling them prevents new process instances from being started through these triggers while preserving the trigger configuration for future re-enablement.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which API triggers to disable. The cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for disabling multiple triggers efficiently. The -Path parameter allows targeting specific folders, and -Recurse enables processing all subfolders.

This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. The -Recurse parameter enables disabling API triggers from all subfolders.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/HttpTriggers/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Write
Required permissions: Triggers.Edit
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that API triggers should be disabled from all subfolders recursively.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the Name of the API triggers to be disabled.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the Name of the API triggers to be disabled.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that API triggers should be disabled from all subfolders recursively.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

API triggers enable external systems to start processes through HTTP API calls. Disabling them temporarily suspends trigger operations while preserving configurations. Use Enable-OrchApiTrigger to re-enable disabled triggers. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

Disables all API triggers containing Integration in their names from all subfolders recursively.
Gets all API triggers containing External in their names and shows what would happen when disabling them using pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables machine account mapping for folder-scoped machines.

The Disable-OrchFolderMachineAccountMapping cmdlet disables the mapping between machines and user accounts within specific folders. This removes the association that allows specific users to connect to machines in the folder context.
This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.
When account mapping is disabled, users will no longer be able to establish dedicated connections to the specified machines through the folder context.

Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineRobots(folderId={folderId},machineId={machineId}), POST /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetMachineRobots
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots
Required permissions: Robots.Edit
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the name of the machines for which account mapping should be disabled.
Specifies the user names for which account mapping should be disabled. If not specified, all account mappings for the specified machines will be disabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the name of the machines for which account mapping should be disabled.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the user names for which account mapping should be disabled. If not specified, all account mappings for the specified machines will be disabled.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Disables inheritance for a FolderMachine, preventing it from propagating to subfolders.

Disables inheritance for a specified FolderMachine, setting the PropagateToSubFolders property to false. This prevents the machine configuration from being inherited by subfolders within the folder hierarchy.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.ToggleFolderMachineInherit
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Write
Required permissions: (Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit - Propagate machine to subfolders only if Units.Edit permission is provided or only if SubFolders.Edit permission on all folders provided)
This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either navigation to the target folder or specification of target folders using -Path parameter.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for FolderMachines. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
`yaml Type: String[] Parameter Sets: (All) Aliases:
Required: False Position: Named Default value: None Accept pipeline input: True (ByPropertyName) Accept wildcard characters: True

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either: - Navigation to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), OR
- Specification of target folders using the -Path parameter Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path before the Name parameter when using both parameters.

The PropagateToSubFolders property determines whether a FolderMachine configuration is inherited by subfolders. When disabled, subfolders within the folder hierarchy will not inherit this machine configuration.
Use Get-OrchFolderMachine to verify the current inheritance status by checking the PropagateToSubFolders property.
For safety, consider using -WhatIf to preview the changes before execution.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables the runtime licenses.
The Disable-OrchLicenseRuntime cmdlet disables runtime licenses for specific robot types and machine keys. Runtime licenses control the ability for robots to execute automation processes.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Disabling runtime licenses will prevent robots from executing new jobs, but running jobs will continue until completion. This is useful for maintenance scenarios or license management.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/LicensesRuntime('{machineName}')/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.ToggleEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.License
Required permissions: Machines.Edit
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the RobotType of the runtime licenses to be disabled. Common values include: Unattended, Attended, Studio, StudioX, NonProduction.
Specifies the Key of the runtime licenses to be disabled. This typically corresponds to machine names or identifiers.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the Key of the runtime licenses to be disabled. This typically corresponds to machine names or identifiers.
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the RobotType of the runtime licenses to be disabled. Common values include: Unattended, Attended, Studio, StudioX, NonProduction.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Disables NonProduction runtime licenses for all machines with keys containing "Test".

maml:description.maml:para: Disables maintenance mode of unattended sessions.

The Disable-OrchMaintenanceMode cmdlet disables maintenance mode for unattended robot sessions within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet allows robot sessions to resume normal operation after maintenance activities, enabling them to accept and execute new jobs.

Maintenance mode is used to temporarily prevent robot sessions from accepting new jobs while allowing currently running jobs to complete. Disabling maintenance mode restores normal robot operation, allowing the sessions to process job queues and accept new automation tasks.

Use the various filtering parameters to target specific robot sessions for maintenance mode disabling. You can filter by MachineName, HostMachineName, ServiceUserName, or specific SessionId values. The -Path parameter allows targeting specific folders.
The -Force parameter can be used to bypass confirmation prompts when disabling maintenance mode for multiple sessions simultaneously.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimes, POST /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetMaintenanceMode
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Write
Required permissions: Robots.Edit
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the machine names to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Specifies the host machine names to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Specifies the service user names to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Specifies the session IDs to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Forces the operation without confirmation prompts.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the host machine names to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Specifies the machine names to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the service user names to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Specifies the session IDs to target for maintenance mode disabling.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: Maintenance mode prevents robot sessions from accepting new jobs while allowing current jobs to complete. Disabling maintenance mode restores normal robot operation. Use filtering parameters to target specific sessions. The -Force parameter bypasses confirmation prompts for bulk operations.

Gets all sessions currently in maintenance mode and shows what would happen when disabling maintenance mode for them using pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables personal workspace access for specified users.

The Disable-OrchPersonalWorkspace cmdlet disables personal workspace functionality for specified users within UiPath Orchestrator. Personal workspaces provide users with dedicated environments for developing, testing, and managing their automation projects independently from shared organizational folders.

Disabling personal workspace prevents users from accessing their individual workspace environments while preserving their existing workspace data. This is useful for enforcing organizational policies, managing resource allocation, temporarily restricting user access during maintenance, or when implementing new security policies.

Internally, this cmdlet calls Update-OrchUser with -MayHavePersonalWorkspace False parameter. Unlike the Enable counterpart, this cmdlet only affects the personal workspace permission and does not modify robot session access. This targeted approach allows for fine-grained control over user capabilities.

This cmdlet operates at the tenant level, managing user workspace access across the organization. Users with disabled personal workspaces will be restricted to shared organizational folders for their automation activities. The operation automatically clears the Orchestrator cache to ensure immediate restriction of workspace access.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Users
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Write
Required permissions: Users.Edit
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations targeting multiple tenants.
Specifies the usernames of users whose personal workspaces should be disabled. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations targeting multiple tenants.
Specifies the usernames of users whose personal workspaces should be disabled. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a tenant-level entity operation that sets the MayHavePersonalWorkspace property to False for specified users. Unlike Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace, this cmdlet only affects workspace access and does not modify robot session permissions. Personal workspaces provide dedicated development environments separate from shared organizational folders. Disabling workspace access restricts users to shared organizational folders while preserving existing workspace data. The operation automatically clears the Orchestrator cache to ensure immediate restriction of workspace access.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables test set schedules in specified folders.

The Disable-OrchTestSetSchedule cmdlet disables test set schedules in specified folders within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet allows you to temporarily suspend scheduled test executions without deleting the schedule configurations, making it useful for maintenance scenarios or when troubleshooting test automation workflows.

Test set schedules automate the execution of test sets at specified intervals or times. Disabling them prevents scheduled test executions from starting while preserving the schedule configuration for future re-enablement.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which test set schedules to disable. The cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for disabling multiple schedules efficiently. The -Path parameter allows targeting specific folders, and -Recurse enables processing all subfolders.

This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. The -Recurse parameter enables disabling test set schedules from all subfolders.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/TestSetSchedules/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSetSchedules or OR.TestSetSchedules.Write
Required permissions: TestSetSchedules.Edit
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that test set schedules should be disabled from all subfolders recursively.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the Name of the test set schedules to be disabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the Name of the test set schedules to be disabled.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that test set schedules should be disabled from all subfolders recursively.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

Test set schedules automate test execution at specified intervals. Disabling them temporarily suspends scheduled test executions while preserving configurations. Use Enable-OrchTestSetSchedule to re-enable disabled schedules. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables specified automation triggers to prevent scheduled execution.

The Disable-OrchTrigger cmdlet disables specified automation triggers within UiPath Orchestrator, preventing them from executing according to their configured schedules or conditions. Triggers define when and how automation processes should be executed, and disabling them temporarily suspends automation execution.

Disabling triggers is useful for maintenance periods, troubleshooting, testing scenarios, or when you need to temporarily halt specific automation processes without deleting the trigger configuration. The trigger settings are preserved and can be easily re-enabled using Enable-OrchTrigger.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Use the -Recurse parameter to include triggers in subfolders, and -Depth to control recursion levels.

Disabling triggers directly affects automation execution schedules. Use -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm for confirmation prompts when disabling multiple triggers that may impact business processes.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/ProcessSchedules/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs
Required permissions: Schedules.Edit
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation. Essential for comprehensive trigger management across folder hierarchies.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the names of triggers to be disabled. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible trigger selection.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. Highly recommended when disabling triggers that affect business processes.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually disabling the triggers. Highly recommended for previewing the operation scope.

Specifies the names of triggers to be disabled. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible trigger selection.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a folder entity operation that suspends automation trigger execution without deleting trigger configuration. Disabling triggers directly affects automation schedules and may impact business processes. Use -WhatIf to preview operations and -Confirm for safety when disabling multiple triggers. The operation requires Schedules.Edit permissions in the target folders. Disabled triggers can be re-enabled using Enable-OrchTrigger while preserving all configuration settings.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables attended robot session capabilities for specified users.

The Disable-OrchUserAttended cmdlet disables attended robot session capabilities for specified users within UiPath Orchestrator. Attended robots run on user desktops and are triggered manually or through user interaction, as opposed to unattended robots that run automatically on server machines.

Disabling attended robot capabilities prevents users from establishing robot sessions and running attended automation processes on their local machines. This effectively blocks attended automation execution while maintaining user access to other Orchestrator features such as viewing jobs, accessing shared folders, and managing assets based on their role permissions.

Internally, this cmdlet calls Update-OrchUser with -MayHaveRobotSession False parameter. This targeted approach specifically controls robot session capabilities without affecting other user permissions like personal workspace access or general Orchestrator functionality.

This cmdlet operates at the tenant level, managing user robot session capabilities across the organization. Unlike its Personal Workspace counterpart, this cmdlet does not automatically clear the cache, making it a lighter operation focused solely on robot session control.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Users
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Write
Required permissions: Users.Edit
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations targeting multiple tenants.
Specifies the usernames of users whose attended robot capabilities should be disabled. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations targeting multiple tenants.
Specifies the usernames of users whose attended robot capabilities should be disabled. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a tenant-level entity operation that sets the MayHaveRobotSession property to False for specified users. Attended robot capabilities allow users to run automation processes on their desktop machines with user interaction. Disabling these capabilities prevents robot session establishment and attended automation execution while preserving other user permissions. This cmdlet operates independently of personal workspace permissions and does not automatically clear the Orchestrator cache.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables attended robot session capabilities for user john.doe in the current tenant.
Shows what would happen when disabling attended capabilities for jane.smith in the Orch1 tenant.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables webhooks in the Orchestrator.

The Disable-OrchWebhook cmdlet disables previously enabled webhooks in the Orchestrator environment. When a webhook is disabled, it will stop sending HTTP notifications to external systems, effectively pausing the integration without removing the webhook configuration.

Disabling webhooks is useful for temporarily stopping notifications during maintenance periods, troubleshooting integration issues, or when external systems are unavailable. The webhook configuration remains intact and can be re-enabled later using the Enable-OrchWebhook cmdlet.
The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before disabling webhooks.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Webhooks({webhookId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks
Required permissions: Webhooks.Edit
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to disable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be disabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be disabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when disabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be disabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when disabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be disabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be disabled.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to disable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be disabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects webhooks across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
Once disabled, webhooks will immediately stop sending notifications. Any events that occur while the webhook is disabled will not be sent to the target URL.
Use Get-OrchWebhook to verify the current status of webhooks before and after disabling them.
Consider using -WhatIf to preview the operation, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple webhooks.
Disabling webhooks is a reversible operation. Use Enable-OrchWebhook to re-enable webhooks when needed.
Temporary disabling of webhooks is preferable to removal when you plan to use the same webhook configuration again in the future.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables the webhook named "IntegrationHook".
Disables multiple webhooks by specifying their names in a comma-separated list.
Shows what would happen if all webhooks whose names start with "Test" were disabled, without actually performing the operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Disable-OrchWebhook MaintenanceHook -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables the webhook named "MaintenanceHook" in multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchWebhook | Where-Object Enabled -eq $true | Disable-OrchWebhook -Confirm

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds all enabled webhooks and disables them with confirmation prompts for each operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Disable-OrchWebhook CriticalEventsHook | Select-Object Name, Enabled, Url

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables a webhook and displays its updated status including name, enabled state, and URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Opens the UiPath Orchestrator configuration file for editing.

The Edit-OrchConfig cmdlet opens the UiPathOrchConfig.json configuration file in the default or specified editor. This file contains the connection configurations for all UiPath Orchestrator drives, including URLs, authentication settings, and other connection parameters.
The configuration file is located at: ([Environment]::GetFolderPath('MyDocuments'))\PowerShell\Modules\UiPathOrch\UiPathOrchConfig.json
Primary Endpoint: N/A (Local file operation)
OAuth required scopes: N/A
Required permissions: File system write access to user documents folder
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet.
Specifies the editor to use for opening the configuration file. Valid values are "Notepad" (uses Windows Notepad) and "Default" (uses the default editor associated with .json files).
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet.
Specifies the editor to use for opening the configuration file. Valid values are "Notepad" (uses Windows Notepad) and "Default" (uses the default editor associated with .json files).

maml:alert.maml:para: After modifying the configuration file: 1. Save the file in the editor 2. Restart PowerShell session 3. Import required modules: Import-Module UiPathOrch, PSReadLine 4. Verify configuration with Get-OrchPSDrive 5. Test connection with Get-OrchCurrentUser
The configuration file contains sensitive information including OAuth settings. Ensure proper file permissions and avoid sharing this file.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 1: Open configuration file in default editor -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the UiPathOrchConfig.json file in the default text editor (typically Notepad on Windows).

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 2: Open with default JSON editor -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the configuration file using the default editor associated with .json files (e.g., Visual Studio Code, Notepad++, or other JSON editors).

maml:description.maml:para: Opens process details for editing in the default web browser.

The `Edit-OrchProcess` cmdlet opens the process details page in the default web browser for editing the specified process(es). This provides a convenient way to navigate from PowerShell to the Orchestrator web interface to modify process settings, view process details, or access editing features not available through the command line.
This cmdlet is useful for quickly navigating from PowerShell to the web interface to edit process configurations, update process settings, or access the visual process designer.
Multiple process names can be specified to open multiple process editing pages simultaneously. You can use autocomplete for process names by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: Browser navigation to process editing page (no direct API endpoint)
OAuth required scopes: Not applicable (browser navigation)
Required permissions: Processes.Edit (for browser access to editing page)
Specifies the target folder path(s). If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.
Specifies the name(s) of the processes to be edited in the browser. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name(s) of the processes to be edited in the browser. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the target folder path(s). If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This is a utility cmdlet for navigating from PowerShell to the Orchestrator web interface
- The cmdlet requires an active web browser and internet connectivity to the Orchestrator instance
- Multiple process names will open multiple browser tabs or windows depending on browser settings
- The user must have appropriate permissions to edit processes in the web interface
- Wildcard patterns are supported for process names

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\MyWorkspace PS Orch1:\MyWorkspace> Edit-OrchProcess MsgBox

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the process editing page for the "MsgBox" process in the default web browser.
Opens process editing pages for multiple processes in separate browser tabs or windows.
Gets all processes with "Agent" in their name and opens their editing pages.
Opens editing pages for all processes whose names start with "My" using wildcard pattern matching.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Edit-OrchProcess -Path Orch1:\Shared, Orch1:\root MsgBox

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the "MsgBox" process editing page from multiple specified folders.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables API triggers in specified folders.

The Enable-OrchApiTrigger cmdlet enables previously disabled API triggers in specified folders within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet reactivates API trigger operations, allowing external systems to initiate UiPath processes through HTTP API calls again.

API triggers enable external systems to start UiPath processes through HTTP API calls. Enabling them restores trigger functionality, allowing new process instances to be started through these triggers. This is typically used after maintenance or troubleshooting activities where triggers were temporarily disabled.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which API triggers to enable. The cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for enabling multiple triggers efficiently. The -Path parameter allows targeting specific folders, and -Recurse enables processing all subfolders.

This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. The -Recurse parameter enables enabling API triggers from all subfolders.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/HttpTriggers/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetEnabled

Required permissions: Triggers.Edit

Specifies the Name of the API triggers to be enabled.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables machine account mapping for folder-scoped machines.

The Enable-OrchFolderMachineAccountMapping cmdlet enables the mapping between machines and user accounts within specific folders. This creates an association that allows specific users to connect to machines in the folder context.
This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.
When account mapping is enabled, the specified users can establish dedicated connections to the specified machines through the folder context, providing controlled access to automation resources.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Enables inheritance for the machine named "ProductionMachine" in the current folder, allowing it to propagate to subfolders.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables the runtime licenses.
The Enable-OrchLicenseRuntime cmdlet enables runtime licenses for specific robot types and machine keys. Runtime licenses control the ability for robots to execute automation processes.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Enabling runtime licenses allows robots to execute automation jobs. This is essential for activating automation capabilities after license allocation or maintenance periods.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/LicensesRuntime('{machineName}')/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.ToggleEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.License
Required permissions: Machines.Edit
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the RobotType of the runtime licenses to be enabled. Common values include: Unattended, Attended, Studio, StudioX, NonProduction.
Specifies the Key of the runtime licenses to be enabled. This typically corresponds to machine names or identifiers.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the Key of the runtime licenses to be enabled. This typically corresponds to machine names or identifiers.
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the RobotType of the runtime licenses to be enabled. Common values include: Unattended, Attended, Studio, StudioX, NonProduction.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows what would happen when enabling the Unattended runtime license for Machine01.
Enables the Unattended runtime license for Machine01 in the current tenant.
Enables Studio and StudioX runtime licenses for all machines whose keys contain "DevMachine".

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Enable-OrchLicenseRuntime -Path Orch1:, Orch2: Unattended ProdMachine01, ProdMachine02 -Confirm

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables Unattended runtime licenses for ProdMachine01 and ProdMachine02 across multiple tenants with confirmation.
Enables NonProduction runtime licenses for all machines with keys containing "Test".
Enables all currently disabled Unattended runtime licenses. License information is passed via pipeline using ByPropertyName binding.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables maintenance mode of unattended sessions.

The Enable-OrchMaintenanceMode cmdlet enables maintenance mode for unattended robot sessions within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet prevents robot sessions from accepting new jobs while allowing currently running jobs to complete gracefully, making it ideal for maintenance activities or system updates.

Maintenance mode is essential for performing maintenance tasks on robot machines without interrupting running automation processes. When enabled, the robot sessions will finish their current jobs but will not accept new jobs from the queue until maintenance mode is disabled.

Use the various filtering parameters to target specific robot sessions for maintenance mode activation. You can filter by MachineName, HostMachineName, ServiceUserName, or specific SessionId values. The -Path parameter allows targeting specific folders.
The -Force parameter can be used to bypass confirmation prompts when enabling maintenance mode for multiple sessions simultaneously.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimes
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Write
Required permissions: Machines.Edit
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the machine names to target for maintenance mode activation.
Specifies the host machine names to target for maintenance mode activation.
Specifies the service user names to target for maintenance mode activation.
Specifies the session IDs to target for maintenance mode activation.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Forces the operation without confirmation prompts.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Forces the operation without confirmation prompts.
Specifies the host machine names to target for maintenance mode activation.
Specifies the machine names to target for maintenance mode activation.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the service user names to target for maintenance mode activation.
Specifies the session IDs to target for maintenance mode activation.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Maintenance mode prevents robot sessions from accepting new jobs while allowing current jobs to complete. This is essential for performing maintenance without interrupting running processes. Use filtering parameters to target specific sessions. The -Force parameter bypasses confirmation prompts for bulk operations.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables maintenance mode for all sessions on machine Robot01 in the current folder.
Enables maintenance mode for all sessions on host machine Server01 in the Production folder.
Shows what would happen when enabling maintenance mode for sessions using ServiceAccount01 and ServiceAccount02.
Enables maintenance mode for specific sessions with IDs 12345 and 67890 in the Development folder without confirmation prompts.
Enables maintenance mode for all sessions on machines with names containing Robot with confirmation prompts.
Gets all sessions not currently in maintenance mode and shows what would happen when enabling maintenance mode for them using pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables personal workspace and robot session access for specified users.

The Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace cmdlet enables both personal workspace functionality and robot session access for specified users within UiPath Orchestrator. This operation grants users comprehensive development and execution capabilities in their individual environments.

Personal workspaces provide users with dedicated environments for developing, testing, and managing their automation projects independently from shared organizational folders. The cmdlet also enables robot session access, allowing users to run attended automation processes within their workspace environment.

Internally, this cmdlet calls Update-OrchUser with both -MayHavePersonalWorkspace True and -MayHaveRobotSession True parameters. This dual configuration ensures users receive both workspace development capabilities and execution permissions, supporting the complete automation development lifecycle.

This cmdlet operates at the tenant level, managing user workspace access across the organization. Users with enabled personal workspaces can access both their dedicated workspace and shared organizational folders. The operation automatically clears the Orchestrator cache to ensure immediate access to the new capabilities.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Users
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Write
Required permissions: Users.Edit
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations targeting multiple tenants.
Specifies the usernames of users whose personal workspaces should be enabled. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations targeting multiple tenants.
Specifies the usernames of users whose personal workspaces should be enabled. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a tenant-level entity operation that sets both MayHavePersonalWorkspace and MayHaveRobotSession properties to True for specified users. Personal workspaces provide dedicated development environments separate from shared organizational folders. The robot session capability allows users to execute attended automation processes within their workspace environment. The operation automatically clears the Orchestrator cache to ensure immediate availability of new permissions.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables personal workspace and robot session access for user john.doe in the current tenant.
Shows what would happen when enabling personal workspace for jane.smith in the Orch1 tenant.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace developer1, developer2, developer3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables personal workspaces for multiple developers in the current tenant.
Enables personal workspaces for all users with usernames containing "citizen" with confirmation prompts.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchUser -Type Internal | Where-Object {$_.Department -eq "RPA"} | Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all internal users from RPA department and enables their personal workspaces using pipeline input.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace -Path Orch1:, Orch2: newuser1, newuser2

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables personal workspaces for newuser1 and newuser2 across multiple tenants.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables test set schedules in specified folders.

The Enable-OrchTestSetSchedule cmdlet enables previously disabled test set schedules in specified folders within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet reactivates scheduled test executions, allowing automated test sets to run according to their configured schedules.

Test set schedules automate the execution of test sets at specified intervals or times. Enabling them restores scheduled test execution functionality after being disabled for maintenance or troubleshooting purposes.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which test set schedules to enable. The cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for enabling multiple schedules efficiently. The -Path parameter allows targeting specific folders, and -Recurse enables processing all subfolders.

This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. The -Recurse parameter enables enabling test set schedules from all subfolders.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/TestSetSchedules/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSetSchedules or OR.TestSetSchedules.Write
Required permissions: TestSetSchedules.Edit
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that test set schedules should be enabled from all subfolders recursively.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the Name of the test set schedules to be enabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the Name of the test set schedules to be enabled.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that test set schedules should be enabled from all subfolders recursively.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

Test set schedules automate test execution at specified intervals. Enabling them restores scheduled test execution functionality after being disabled. Use Disable-OrchTestSetSchedule to temporarily suspend schedule operations. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the RegressionTestSchedule in the current folder (Development) using positional parameters.
Enables the SmokeTestSchedule in the Orch1:\Development folder.
Shows what would happen when enabling multiple test set schedules with names containing Daily or Weekly in the current folder using -WhatIf for safety.
Enables all test set schedules containing Automated in their name in the Development folder with confirmation prompts.
Enables all test set schedules containing Nightly in their names from all subfolders recursively.
Gets all disabled test set schedules and shows what would happen when enabling them using pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables specified automation triggers to resume scheduled execution.

The Enable-OrchTrigger cmdlet enables specified automation triggers within UiPath Orchestrator, allowing them to resume scheduled execution of automation processes. Triggers define when and how automation processes should be executed, including time-based schedules, queue-based triggers, and other automation activation conditions.

When triggers are enabled, they become active and will execute according to their configured schedule or conditions. This cmdlet is essential for resuming automation execution after maintenance periods, troubleshooting, or when triggers have been temporarily disabled.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Use the -Recurse parameter to include triggers in subfolders, and -Depth to control recursion levels.

Enabling triggers is a critical operation that directly affects automation execution schedules. Use -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm for confirmation prompts when enabling multiple triggers.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/ProcessSchedules/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.SetEnabled
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs
Required permissions: Schedules.Edit
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation. Essential for comprehensive trigger management across folder hierarchies.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the names of triggers to be enabled. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible trigger selection.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. Recommended when enabling multiple triggers that affect automation schedules.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually enabling the triggers. Recommended for previewing the operation scope.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the names of triggers to be enabled. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible trigger selection.
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation. Essential for comprehensive trigger management across folder hierarchies.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. Recommended when enabling multiple triggers that affect automation schedules.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually enabling the triggers. Recommended for previewing the operation scope.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a folder entity operation that activates automation triggers for scheduled execution. Enabling triggers directly affects automation schedules and process execution. Use -WhatIf to preview operations and -Confirm for safety when enabling multiple triggers. The operation requires Schedules.Edit permissions in the target folders. Once enabled, triggers will execute according to their configured schedules and conditions.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the DailyReportTrigger in the current Production folder.
Shows what would happen when enabling all triggers with names containing "Invoice" in the Production folder.
Enables MonthlyTrigger and WeeklyTrigger across all folders with confirmation prompts.
Enables multiple test triggers in the Development folder.
Enables all triggers with names containing "Schedule" in the Production folder and up to 2 levels of subfolders.
Shows what would happen when enabling all currently disabled triggers using pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables attended automation for specified users.

The Enable-OrchUserAttended cmdlet enables attended automation capabilities for specified users in the Orchestrator environment. Attended automation allows users to work collaboratively with robots, executing processes in their user sessions while maintaining user interaction and supervision.

When enabled, users can run robots in attended mode, which means the robots execute in the user's active session and can interact with applications and interfaces that the user has access to. This is particularly useful for scenarios where human oversight or interaction is required during process execution.

The cmdlet requires the UserName parameter to specify which users should have attended automation enabled. Multiple users can be specified simultaneously. The operation affects user permissions and capabilities within the Orchestrator tenant.
This setting is essential for organizations implementing attended automation scenarios where users and robots collaborate to complete business processes efficiently.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users, GET /odata/Users({userId}), PUT /odata/Users({userId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Users.Edit
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the usernames for which attended automation should be enabled. Multiple usernames can be provided to enable attended automation for multiple users simultaneously.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the usernames for which attended automation should be enabled. Multiple usernames can be provided to enable attended automation for multiple users simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: Attended automation must be properly licensed and configured in your Orchestrator environment before users can utilize this capability.

This cmdlet modifies user permissions and capabilities. Ensure that users have the appropriate licenses and that your organization's security policies allow attended automation for the specified users.

Attended automation enables users to run robots in their active sessions, which means the robots will have the same access permissions as the user. Consider security implications when enabling this feature.
The opposite operation can be performed using Disable-OrchUserAttended to revoke attended automation capabilities from users.
Use -WhatIf to preview changes before applying them, especially when enabling attended automation for multiple users.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables attended automation for the user "john.doe". Uses positional parameter for the username.
Enables attended automation for multiple users simultaneously. This allows both alice.smith and bob.jones to run robots in attended mode.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Enable-OrchUserAttended -UserName marketing.team -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables attended automation for the marketing.team user across multiple Orchestrator instances. Useful in multi-tenant environments.
Shows what would happen if attended automation was enabled for admin.user without actually making the change. Useful for verification before execution.
Finds all users with "automation" in their username and enables attended automation for them with confirmation prompts for each user.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables the webhooks.

The Enable-OrchWebhook cmdlet enables previously disabled webhooks in the Orchestrator environment. Webhooks allow Orchestrator to send HTTP notifications to external systems when specific events occur, enabling real-time integration with external applications and services.

When a webhook is enabled, it will start sending notifications for the events it is configured to monitor. This includes events such as job completion, process deployment, robot status changes, and other Orchestrator activities based on the webhook's configuration.
The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before enabling webhooks.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Webhooks({webhookId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks
Required permissions: Webhooks.Edit
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to enable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be enabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be enabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when enabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be enabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when enabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be enabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be enabled.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to enable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be enabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects webhooks across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.

Once enabled, webhooks will immediately start sending notifications based on their configured event types and filters. Ensure that the target URLs are accessible and ready to receive webhook notifications before enabling them.
Use Get-OrchWebhook to verify the current status of webhooks before and after enabling them.
Consider using -WhatIf to preview the operation, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple webhooks.
If a webhook's target URL is no longer valid or accessible, enabling it may result in failed notification attempts and potential performance impacts.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the webhook named "JobCompletionHook".
Enables multiple webhooks by specifying their names in a comma-separated list.
Shows what would happen if all webhooks whose names start with "Test" were enabled, without actually performing the operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Enable-OrchWebhook ProductionHook -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the webhook named "ProductionHook" in multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchWebhook | Where-Object Enabled -eq $false | Enable-OrchWebhook -Confirm

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds all disabled webhooks and enables them with confirmation prompts for each operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Enable-OrchWebhook CriticalEventsHook | Select-Object Name, Enabled, Url

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables a webhook and displays its updated status including name, enabled state, and URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports job media files from specified folders to local files.

The Export-OrchJobMedia cmdlet exports media files (screenshots, recordings, logs) associated with job executions from UiPath Orchestrator folders to local storage. This cmdlet enables backup, archival, or detailed analysis of job execution artifacts for troubleshooting, compliance, or audit purposes.

Job media includes execution screenshots, video recordings, execution logs, and other diagnostic files generated during automation job runs. Exporting these files allows for offline analysis, long-term archival, or sharing with support teams for troubleshooting purposes.

Use the -JobId parameter to specify which jobs' media files to export. The -Destination parameter specifies where the exported media files should be saved. The cmdlet supports exporting media from multiple jobs and organizing files by job ID in the destination directory.

This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. The -Recurse parameter enables exporting job media from all subfolders.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/ExecutionMedia
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs or OR.Jobs.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the source folders. If not specified, the current folder will be used as the source.
Specifies that job media files should be exported from all subfolders recursively.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the job IDs whose media files should be exported. If not specified, media files for all jobs will be exported.
Specifies the destination directory where exported media files will be saved. If not specified, files will be saved to the current directory.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the maximum number of subfolder levels to include when using -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the destination directory where exported media files will be saved. If not specified, files will be saved to the current directory.
Specifies the job IDs whose media files should be exported. If not specified, media files for all jobs will be exported.
Specifies the source folders. If not specified, the current folder will be used as the source.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that job media files should be exported from all subfolders recursively.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

Job media files include screenshots, video recordings, and execution logs. Export operations may take time for large media files or multiple jobs. Ensure sufficient disk space for media exports. Media files are organized by job ID in the destination directory. Use -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports all media files for job ID 12345 from the current folder to the default destination.
Exports all media files for job ID 67890 from the Production folder to C:\JobMedia directory.
Shows what would happen when exporting media files for multiple job IDs from the current folder to C:\Exports directory.
Exports all job media files from the Development folder and all its subfolders to C:\AllJobMedia directory.
Exports all job media files from the current folder and up to 2 levels of subfolders to C:\BackupMedia with confirmation prompts.
Gets all failed jobs and exports their media files to C:\FailedJobs using pipeline input for troubleshooting purposes.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports libraries from tenants to local files.

The Export-OrchLibrary cmdlet exports reusable component libraries from UiPath Orchestrator tenants to local .nupkg files. This cmdlet enables backup, archival, or migration of activity libraries between different Orchestrator environments or for offline storage.

Libraries contain reusable activities, custom activities, and workflow components that can be shared across multiple automation projects. Exporting libraries creates .nupkg files that can be stored locally, transferred to other environments, or used for backup purposes. This is essential for component management, version control, and environment migration scenarios.

Use the -Id parameter to specify which libraries to export by their library ID. The -Version parameter allows targeting specific library versions, and -Destination specifies where the exported .nupkg files should be saved. The cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for exporting multiple libraries efficiently.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies the source tenant drives (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:) from which libraries should be exported.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries, GET /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetVersions, GET /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.DownloadPackage
OAuth required scopes: OR.Assets or OR.Assets.Read
Required permissions: Assets.View
Specifies the source tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be used as the source.
Specifies the library IDs to be exported. If not specified, all libraries will be exported.
Specifies the library versions to be exported. If not specified, the latest version will be exported.
Specifies the destination directory where exported .nupkg files will be saved. If not specified, files will be saved to the current directory.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the destination directory where exported .nupkg files will be saved. If not specified, files will be saved to the current directory.
Specifies the library IDs to be exported. If not specified, all libraries will be exported.
Specifies the source tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be used as the source.

Exported libraries are saved as .nupkg files that can be imported into other Orchestrator environments using Import-OrchLibrary. Libraries contain reusable activities and components. Ensure sufficient disk space for large libraries. Use version wildcards for exporting multiple versions. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.
