maml:description.maml:para: Gets connection string.

The Get-OrchConnectionString cmdlet retrieves the connection string used by the Orchestrator service to connect to its database. This information is useful for system administrators who need to verify database connectivity settings, perform troubleshooting, or understand the current Orchestrator configuration.

Connection strings contain critical information about the database server, database name, authentication method, and other connection parameters. This cmdlet provides read-only access to this configuration data.

The cmdlet returns a UiPath.PowerShell.Entities.ODataValueOfString object that wraps the actual connection string value. Use the Value property or Select-Object -ExpandProperty Value to access the raw connection string.

This is typically used for diagnostic purposes and system administration tasks. The connection string information can help in troubleshooting connectivity issues or verifying that Orchestrator is configured to use the correct database.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet provides read-only access to the Orchestrator database connection string. The connection string information is sensitive and should be handled securely according to your organization's security policies.
The returned value is wrapped in a UiPath.PowerShell.Entities.ODataValueOfString object. Use the Value property or Select-Object -ExpandProperty Value to extract just the connection string text.

Connection strings typically contain sensitive information such as database server locations, database names, and authentication details. Ensure appropriate access controls are in place when using this cmdlet.
This cmdlet is primarily intended for system administrators and troubleshooting purposes. Regular users typically do not need access to connection string information.
The cmdlet operates on tenant-level settings and requires appropriate Settings.View permissions to access configuration data.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetConnectionString OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the connection string for the current Orchestrator instance. Returns a UiPath.PowerShell.Entities.ODataValueOfString object containing the database connection information.
Retrieves connection strings from multiple Orchestrator instances. This is useful when managing multiple environments and you need to verify the database configuration for each.
Gets the connection string and displays only the actual connection string value, excluding the wrapper object properties. This provides direct access to the raw connection string text.
Stores the connection string object in a variable and then accesses the actual connection string value. This approach is useful when you need to further process or analyze the connection string.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves credential stores configured in UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchCredentialStore cmdlet retrieves credential stores configured within UiPath Orchestrator. Credential stores provide secure external storage for sensitive credentials, enabling integration with external secret management systems such as AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, and database-based credential storage.

Each credential store contains properties such as Name, Type (storage provider), ProxyId, ProxyType, HostName, AdditionalConfiguration (provider-specific settings), and read-only status. These stores serve as secure repositories for automation credentials used by robots and processes.

This cmdlet operates as a tenant-level entity operation, retrieving credential stores from the specified Orchestrator environment. The AdditionalConfiguration property contains provider-specific settings with sensitive values masked for security.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/CredentialStores
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations.
Specifies the names of credential stores to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible store selection.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies the names of credential stores to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible store selection.
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a tenant-level entity operation for accessing credential store configurations. Credential stores provide secure external storage for automation credentials with various provider types including AWS Secrets Manager and database storage. Sensitive configuration values are masked in the output for security. This operation requires Settings.View permissions.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/CredentialStores OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all credential stores, displaying Id, Name, Type, ProxyId, ProxyType, HostName, IsReadOnly, and masked AdditionalConfiguration.
Displays detailed credential store properties in JSON format, including all configuration details with sensitive values masked.
Gets all credential stores with names starting with "AWS" in the Orch1 tenant.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCredentialStore | Where-Object {$_.Type -eq "AWS Secrets Manager"}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all AWS Secrets Manager credential stores.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCredentialStore | Where-Object {$_.Details.IsReadOnly -eq $false} | Select-Object Name, Type

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves only editable credential stores (not read-only) and displays their names and types for management purposes.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCredentialStore | Group-Object Type | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups credential stores by provider type and displays the count for each type, providing an overview of store distribution.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the user connected to the tenant of the target drive.

The `Get-OrchCurrentUser` cmdlet retrieves information about the currently authenticated user for the specified Orchestrator drive. This includes user details such as ID, username, full name, email address, user type, and assigned roles.

This cmdlet can only be used for drives connected with non-confidential application settings (user-scoped authentication). It cannot be used with confidential application drives (app-scoped authentication).
The current user may be different for each drive when multiple Orchestrator instances are configured with different authentication contexts.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Users/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetCurrentUser
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - requires verification]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Multiple drive paths can be specified to check the current user across different Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Multiple drive paths can be specified to check the current user across different Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet only works with non-confidential application connections (user-scoped authentication)
- Confidential application drives (app-scoped authentication) do not support this operation
- The current user may be different for each configured drive
- The RolesList property contains all roles assigned to the current user
- The Type property indicates the user account type (e.g., DirectoryUser)

Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetCurrentUserExtended OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - requires verification] Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - requires verification]

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\ PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCurrentUser

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the current user information for the current Orchestrator drive.
Gets the current user information for multiple Orchestrator drives to compare authentication contexts.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $user = Get-OrchCurrentUser PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Current user: $($user.FullName) ($($user.EmailAddress))" PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Roles: $($user.RolesList -join ', ')"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the current user and displays formatted information about the user and their roles.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCurrentUser | Select-Object UserName, FullName, EmailAddress, Type, RolesList

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the current user and displays only the key properties.
Gets the current user information and displays it in detailed list format.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves execution settings configured in UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchExecutionSetting cmdlet retrieves execution settings configured within UiPath Orchestrator. Execution settings control various aspects of automation execution behavior, including logging levels, console access, resolution settings, and development features.

Settings are organized by scope (Global and Robot) and contain properties such as DisplayName, ValueType (Boolean, Integer, MultipleChoice), DefaultValue, and PossibleValues for enumeration types. These settings directly affect how automation processes execute and behave in the Orchestrator environment.

This cmdlet operates as a tenant-level entity operation, retrieving settings from the specified Orchestrator environment. The output is grouped by scope, showing all available execution configuration options with their current values and constraints.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations.
Specifies the scope of execution settings to retrieve. Valid values are 'Global' and 'Robot'. If not specified, settings from all scopes are returned.
Specifies the display names of execution settings to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible setting selection.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies the display names of execution settings to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible setting selection.
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations.
Specifies the scope of execution settings to retrieve. Valid values are 'Global' and 'Robot'. If not specified, settings from all scopes are returned.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a tenant-level entity operation for accessing execution setting configurations. Settings control automation execution behavior including logging, console access, and resolution settings. Output is grouped by scope showing Global and Robot-level settings. Use filtering to find specific settings by key or value type. This operation requires Settings.View permissions.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetExecutionSettingsConfiguration OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all execution settings grouped by scope (Global and Robot), displaying DisplayName, ValueType, DefaultValue, and PossibleValues.
Displays detailed execution setting properties in JSON format, including Key, Scope, PathScope, and all configuration details.
Gets all execution settings with keys containing "Logging" in the Orch1 tenant.
Retrieves all boolean-type execution settings.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchExecutionSetting | Where-Object {$_.PossibleValues.Count -gt 0} | Select-Object DisplayName, PossibleValues

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays settings with enumerated values (MultipleChoice type) and their possible options.
Groups execution settings by their scope (Global vs Robot).

maml:description.maml:para: Get the machines assigned to folders.

Outputs information about machines with specified names that are assigned to the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no machine names are specified, it outputs all machines assigned to the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachinesForFolder(key={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: (Units.View or SubFolders.View - Gets machines for any folder or only if user has SubFolders.View permission on folder)
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the Name of the folder machines to be retrieved.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies the encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. The default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports the retrieved folder machines to a CSV file with the specified path. The CSV includes human-readable names instead of internal IDs and uses UTF-8 encoding with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the Name of the folder machines to be retrieved.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. The default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports the retrieved folder machines to a CSV file with the specified path. The CSV includes human-readable names instead of internal IDs and uses UTF-8 encoding with BOM for Excel compatibility.

maml:alert.maml:para: Main endpoint called: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachinesForFolder({folderId})
Required Scope: OR.Folders.Read

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all machines assigned to the 'Shared' folder, which is the current location.
Displays all machines assigned to the current folder and all its subfolders. When run in the root folder, it shows all machines assigned across all folders in that tenant.
Displays machines with names starting with 'Machine' assigned to the current folder and its subfolders. This is useful for identifying which folders contain machines with specific name patterns.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderMachine -Path Orch1:\\Production, Orch1:\\Development Template*

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays machines with names starting with 'Template' assigned to the Production and Development folders.
Displays all the machines assigned to Orch1: and Orch2:.
Displays output with only the selected columns. Specify multiple columns separated by commas, including wildcards. Column names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

Exports the output to a CSV file. The CSV file will be located at the current location of the C: drive. You can customize the CSV format by combining with `select` to specify which columns to include. Try `ii c:' to open the current location of the C: drive.
Converts the output to JSON format, providing a raw view of the data from Orchestrator.
Exports all folder machine assignments to a CSV file for reporting or backup purposes. The CSV includes human-readable names and folder assignments.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machine account mappings for folder-scoped machines.

The Get-OrchFolderMachineAccountMapping cmdlet retrieves the account mappings between machines and user accounts within specific folders. These mappings define which users can connect to which machines in the folder context.
This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.
The cmdlet returns information about machine-user relationships, including robot accounts and their associated machines within the folder scope.

Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineRobots(folderId={folderId},machineId={machineId}), GET /odata/Robots/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetFolderRobots(folderId={folderId},machineId={machineId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read
Required permissions: (SubFolders.View or Units.View or Jobs.Create)
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the name of the machines for which account mappings should be retrieved.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the name of the machines for which account mappings should be retrieved.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet retrieves machine account mappings that define which users can connect to specific machines within folder contexts. The mapping information includes robot accounts and their associated machines, which is essential for security and access control.

Machine account mappings are folder-scoped, meaning the same machine may have different account mappings in different folders. This cmdlet requires appropriate folder permissions to access account mapping information.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachinesForFolder OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Read Required permissions: Machines.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine account mappings in the current folder.
Gets account mappings for the machine named "Machine01" in the current folder.
Gets account mappings for all machines whose names contain "Prod".
Gets all machine account mappings from the current folder and all its subfolders.
Gets machine account mappings from both Development and Testing folders.
Gets all machine account mappings where the robot user name contains "service".
Gets account mappings for all machines containing "Robot". Machine names are passed via pipeline using ByPropertyName binding.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets folder usage statistics showing entity counts in UiPath Orchestrator folders.

The Get-OrchFolderUsage cmdlet retrieves comprehensive usage statistics for UiPath Orchestrator folders, showing counts of various entities such as processes, robots, queues, assets, and other resources contained within each folder.

This cmdlet provides valuable insights for resource management, capacity planning, and organizational analysis by displaying how automation resources are distributed across the folder hierarchy. It can help identify heavily utilized folders, unused resources, and optimize resource allocation.

The cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive traversal to analyze entire folder hierarchies. It returns detailed statistics including entity types, counts, and folder paths, making it useful for auditing and reporting purposes.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetEntityUsage(folderId={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: Folders.View

Specifies the folder paths where to search for usage information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Recursively gets usage statistics from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for usage information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Recursively gets usage statistics from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
The usage statistics help identify resource distribution patterns and can be valuable for: - Capacity planning and resource optimization
- Identifying unused or underutilized folders
- Monitoring resource growth and usage trends
- Organizational analysis and reporting
For performance analysis and reporting, consider exporting results to CSV format or using Select-Object to focus on specific metrics.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetEntitiesSummary OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read Required permissions: Units.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets usage statistics for the current folder.
Gets usage statistics for all folders recursively starting from the current location.
Gets usage statistics for all folders under the Production folder recursively.
Gets usage statistics for folders up to 2 levels deep from the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderUsage -Recurse | Select-Object Path, Type, Count | Sort-Object Count -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets usage statistics and displays them sorted by count in descending order.
Retrieves usage statistics and displays the complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderUsage -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.Type -eq "QueueItem" -and $_.Count -gt 1000} | Select-Object Path, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds folders with high queue item counts (over 1000) for capacity planning and queue management.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderUsage -Recurse | Group-Object Category | Select-Object Name, Count, @{Name="TotalItems";Expression={(.Group | Measure-Object Count -Sum).Sum}}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups usage statistics by category and shows total item counts for each category across all folders. This is useful for getting an overview of resource distribution by type.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves users and groups assigned to folders in UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchFolderUser cmdlet retrieves users and groups assigned to folders within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet provides visibility into folder-level access control, showing which users and groups have access to specific folders and their assigned roles and permissions.

Each folder user entry contains information about the user entity (including Type: DirectoryUser or DirectoryGroup), UserName, HasAlertsEnabled status, RobotType capabilities (Attended, Unattended), and assigned Roles with their permissions. The UserEntity includes details such as FullName, AuthenticationSource, and robot capabilities.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Use the -Recurse parameter to include users from subfolders, and -Depth to control recursion levels.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/FolderUsers

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a folder entity operation requiring navigation to a folder context or path specification using -Path parameter. The cmdlet reveals folder-level access control showing both individual users (DirectoryUser) and groups (DirectoryGroup) with their role assignments. UserEntity.Type distinguishes between users and groups. Roles include Origin information showing whether permissions are directly "Assigned" or inherited. This operation requires Users.View permissions in the target folders.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetUsersForFolder OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read Required permissions: Users.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all users and groups assigned to the current Shared folder, displaying Id, UserEntity.Type, UserEntity.UserName, HasAlertsEnabled, RobotType, and Roles.
Displays detailed folder user properties in JSON format, including complete UserEntity details and Role information with Origin and InheritedFromFolder properties.
Gets all folder users with usernames containing "admin" in the Shared folder.
Retrieves all individual users (not groups) assigned across all folders using the -Type parameter for efficient filtering.
Displays detailed folder user properties in JSON format, including complete UserEntity details and Role information with Origin and InheritedFromFolder properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchFolderUser -Recurse | Group-Object {$_.UserEntity.Type} | Select-Object Name, Count, @{Name="UserNames";Expression={($_.Group.UserEntity.UserName | Sort-Object -Unique) -join ", "}}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups folder users by entity type and displays user counts with actual usernames for each type, providing a clear overview of user distribution.

maml:description.maml:para: Displays documentation and quick start guide for the UiPathOrch module.

The Get-OrchHelp cmdlet displays documentation and quick start guide for the UiPathOrch module. This command provides immediate access to module information, available resources, and guidance for getting started with UiPath Orchestrator automation.
The cmdlet serves as a centralized help system that directs users to appropriate documentation, reference materials, and essential commands needed to work effectively with the UiPathOrch module.

This cmdlet is particularly valuable for new users who need orientation with the module's capabilities, developers integrating with UiPath Orchestrator, and automated systems that require structured guidance about module usage patterns.
The command returns formatted text that provides an overview of available resources and recommended next steps for working with the UiPathOrch module.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is particularly useful for users who are new to the UiPathOrch module and need guidance on available resources and recommended practices.
The cmdlet provides a centralized help system that serves as a starting point for module exploration and learning.
For programmatic access or integration scenarios, the output can be captured and processed as needed.
This cmdlet does not require any parameters and can be executed immediately after module import to provide orientation and guidance.
The help content is designed to be self-contained and provides direction to additional resources without requiring external dependencies.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays the UiPathOrch module help and documentation guide. This provides an overview of available resources and getting-started information.
Displays the help information using Out-Host to ensure proper formatting in the console, useful in scripts where output formatting is important.
Captures the help content in a variable for further processing or analysis. This approach allows you to work with the help text programmatically.
Saves the help content to a text file for offline reference or documentation purposes.
Extracts specific sections from the help content, such as the essential commands section. This is useful for automated scripts that need to access specific guidance information.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets jobs.
Gets job information from UiPath Orchestrator folders. Jobs represent process executions and contain details about automation runs including status, timing, robot assignments, and execution results.

This cmdlet provides powerful filtering capabilities to query jobs by various criteria such as time ranges, status, process names, robots, and priorities. It operates on folder entities and supports recursive retrieval across folder hierarchies.
Multiple values for parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves job media files (screenshots, videos, and recordings) from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchJobMedia cmdlet retrieves job media files including screenshots, videos, and recordings from UiPath Orchestrator. Job media provides visual documentation of automation execution, enabling debugging, audit trails, and process verification through captured images and videos.

Media files are associated with specific job executions and contain information such as file type, creation time, file size, and download links. This cmdlet helps access visual evidence of automation execution for troubleshooting, compliance, and process optimization purposes.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Use the -Recurse parameter to include media from jobs in subfolders, and -Depth to control recursion levels.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Jobs/{jobKey}/Media
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the search operation. Essential for comprehensive job media discovery.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.

Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a folder entity operation for accessing job media files including screenshots, videos, and recordings. Job media provides visual documentation of automation execution for debugging, auditing, and process verification. Media files are associated with specific job executions and may have retention policies affecting availability. Use JobKey parameter to target specific jobs or filter by time ranges for recent executions. This operation requires Jobs.View permissions in the target folders.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER] OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER] Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER]

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all job media files from the current Shared folder, displaying basic properties like MediaType, FileName, and CreatedTime.
Displays detailed job media properties in JSON format, including complete file information and metadata structure.
Retrieves job media from the Production folder and subfolders created in the last 7 days.
Retrieves the first 10 job media files across all folders using the -First parameter for performance optimization.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJobMedia | Where-Object {$_.MediaType -eq "Screenshot"} | Select-Object FileName, FileSize, CreatedTime

dev:remarks.maml:para: Filters for screenshot media files and displays key properties. Uses Where-Object since no dedicated MediaType parameter exists.
Calculates the total file size of all job media across folders.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets job statistics from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchJobStats` cmdlet retrieves statistical information about jobs in UiPath Orchestrator. It returns a count of jobs grouped by their execution status (Successful, Faulted, Stopped, Running, Pending, Stopping, Terminating, Suspended, Resumed).
Job statistics are tenant entities that operate across the entire tenant scope. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants by drive name (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:).
This cmdlet provides a quick overview of job execution status across the Orchestrator environment, which is useful for monitoring and reporting purposes.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetJobsStats
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - The statistics include all job statuses: Successful, Faulted, Stopped, Running, Pending, Stopping, Terminating, Suspended, and Resumed
- The hasPermissions property indicates whether the current user has permission to view the job count for each status
- This cmdlet provides a snapshot of current job statistics at the time of execution
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetJobsStats OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER] Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER]

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchJobStats

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets job statistics for the current Orchestrator instance, showing the count of jobs by status.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $stats = Get-OrchJobStats PS Orch1:\> $stats | Where-Object {$_.count -gt 0}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets job statistics and filters to show only statuses that have jobs with non-zero counts.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchJobStats | Sort-Object count -Descending | Select-Object Path, title, count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets job statistics and displays them sorted by count in descending order, showing only the title and count properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $total = (Get-OrchJobStats | Measure-Object count -Sum).Sum PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Total jobs: $total"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Calculates and displays the total number of jobs across all statuses.
Gets job statistics from multiple tenants.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets jobs with video recordings attached.

The Get-OrchJobVideo cmdlet retrieves jobs that have video recordings attached from the Orchestrator environment. This cmdlet is useful for monitoring and auditing job executions where screen recording was enabled, providing visual evidence of automation processes for debugging, compliance, or training purposes.
This cmdlet is implemented as a function that calls the Get-OrchJob cmdlet and searches for jobs from the past week that have a video attached.

Video recordings are captured during job execution when the appropriate recording settings are configured in Orchestrator. These recordings provide valuable insights into robot behavior, UI interactions, and process execution flow.

The cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you must either navigate to a target folder using Set-Location (cd) or specify target folders using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. This allows you to search for jobs with video recordings across different organizational folders.

The cmdlet supports pagination through -Skip and -First parameters to manage large result sets efficiently. Use -Recurse to search through subfolder hierarchies for a comprehensive view of video-enabled job executions.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Jobs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs or OR.Jobs.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet requires navigation to a specific folder or use of the -Path parameter to specify target folders. It operates on folder entities within the Orchestrator hierarchy.

Video recording must be enabled in Orchestrator settings and configured for specific processes to capture job execution videos. If no results are returned, verify that video recording is enabled and that jobs have been executed with recording active.
The cmdlet is particularly useful for: - Debugging failed automation processes
- Training and documentation purposes
- Compliance and audit requirements
- Process optimization through visual analysis
Use -Recurse to search through nested folder structures, and -First/-Skip for pagination when dealing with large numbers of recorded jobs.
Video files are typically stored in Orchestrator's media storage and can be accessed through the returned job objects for download or viewing.

Primary Endpoint: Function implementation (uses Get-OrchJob which calls GET /odata/Jobs) OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - requires verification] Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - requires verification]

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all jobs with video recordings from the current folder. Requires navigation to a specific folder before execution.
Displays detailed job video properties in JSON format, showing complete job information and video metadata structure.
Recursively searches for jobs with video recordings across all folders in the Orch1: drive, including subfolders.
Retrieves the first 10 jobs with video recordings from the Production folder. Useful for managing large result sets.
Retrieves jobs with video recordings from multiple specified folders simultaneously.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets library packages from Orchestrator or external host feeds.

Gets library packages from UiPath Orchestrator tenants or external host feeds. Libraries are reusable components that contain activities, workflows, and other automation resources that can be shared across multiple processes.

By default, this cmdlet returns libraries from the connected Orchestrator instance. When the -HostFeed parameter is specified, it retrieves packages from external host feeds (such as the official NuGet gallery and UiPath feeds) that can be imported into Orchestrator.
Libraries are tenant entities that operate across the entire tenant scope. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants by drive name.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Libraries.View
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Specifies the IDs of libraries to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Retrieves libraries from external host feeds instead of the local Orchestrator instance. This shows libraries available for import from NuGet gallery and UiPath feeds.
Specifies the IDs of libraries to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Retrieves libraries from external host feeds instead of the local Orchestrator instance. This shows libraries available for import from NuGet gallery and UiPath feeds.

maml:alert.maml:para: Library entities are tenant-scoped and operate across the entire tenant.
Use -HostFeed to browse libraries available for import from external sources before using Import-OrchLibrary to add them to your Orchestrator.
Libraries from host feeds show available packages that can be imported, while local libraries show packages already available in the Orchestrator.
The Published property shows when the library was last published, useful for identifying recent updates.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read Required permissions: Libraries.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all libraries from the current tenant.
Gets libraries containing "Excel" in their ID.
Gets available libraries from external host feeds.
Gets libraries containing "Activities" in their ID from host feeds.
Gets libraries from multiple tenants.
Gets libraries published in the last 30 days.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLibrary -HostFeed | Where-Object {$_.Id -like "*UiPath*"} | Select-Object Id, Version, Description

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets UiPath libraries from host feeds with selected properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets specific versions of library packages from Orchestrator or external host feeds.

The Get-OrchLibraryVersion cmdlet retrieves specific versions of library packages from the Orchestrator environment or external host feeds. Unlike Get-OrchLibrary which returns the latest version of libraries, this cmdlet allows you to query all available versions of a specific library or retrieve a particular version.

This cmdlet is particularly useful for version management, dependency analysis, and when you need to work with specific versions of libraries for compatibility purposes. It can help identify all available versions of a library, check version history, and retrieve specific versions for deployment or rollback scenarios.
When used with the -HostFeed parameter, it can query external package repositories to find available versions before importing them into Orchestrator.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetVersions(packageId='libraryId}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Libraries.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to query library versions from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.

Specifies the ID of the libraries whose versions will be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When specified without -Version, returns all available versions of the matching libraries.
Specifies the version(s) of the libraries to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter to filter for specific version patterns or exact versions.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

When specified, retrieves library versions from external host feeds instead of the Orchestrator library repository. This allows checking available versions of packages that can be imported from external sources such as the official NuGet gallery.

Specifies the ID of the libraries whose versions will be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When specified without -Version, returns all available versions of the matching libraries.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to query library versions from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the version(s) of the libraries to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter to filter for specific version patterns or exact versions.

When specified, retrieves library versions from external host feeds instead of the Orchestrator library repository. This allows checking available versions of packages that can be imported from external sources such as the official NuGet gallery.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it retrieves information from the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
When using the -HostFeed parameter, be aware that this retrieves information from external sources and may take longer to complete depending on network conditions and the size of the external feed.
This cmdlet is particularly useful for version management and dependency analysis. Use it to: - Check all available versions before upgrading or downgrading libraries
- Identify prerelease versions for testing purposes
- Analyze version history and release patterns
- Verify specific versions are available before deployment
The returned objects include IsPrerelease property to distinguish between stable and prerelease versions.

Use ConvertTo-Json with appropriate depth settings to explore the complete structure of library version objects, as they contain detailed metadata that may not be visible in the default display format.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetVersions OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read Required permissions: Libraries.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all available versions of the "UiPath.Excel.Activities" library.
Displays all versions of "UiPath.Excel.Activities" library with detailed properties in JSON format for structure analysis.
Retrieves all versions of "MyLibrary" that match the pattern "1.0.*" (all 1.0.x versions).
Retrieves all available versions of "UiPath.System.Activities" from external host feeds.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion -Path Orch1:, Orch2: MyCustomLibrary

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all versions of "MyCustomLibrary" from multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion MyLibrary | Sort-Object Version -Descending | Select-Object -First 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all versions of "MyLibrary" and displays the 5 most recent versions.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion MyLibrary | Where-Object {$_.IsPrerelease -eq $false} | Select-Object Id, Version, Published

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves only stable (non-prerelease) versions of "MyLibrary" and displays key version information.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the license information from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchLicense` cmdlet retrieves detailed license information from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes license expiration dates, grace period information, allowed and used license counts for different license types (Unattended, Attended, StudioPro, etc.), subscription details, and other licensing features.
The license information is essential for understanding the current licensing status, tracking usage, and ensuring compliance with UiPath licensing terms.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicense
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - License view permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - The ExpireDate and GracePeriodEndDate are returned as Unix timestamps
- The Allowed and Used properties contain hashtables with license counts by type (Unattended, Attended, StudioPro, etc.)
- License types include: ProcessOrchestration, AppTest, Headless, Development, StudioX, NonProduction, StudioPro, TestAutomation, Unattended, AgentService, Attended, and Hosting
- The IsExpired property indicates current license validity status
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicense OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the complete license information for the current Orchestrator instance.
Retrieves the license information and displays the allowed license counts by type.
Gets license information from multiple tenants.
Gets license information and displays the complete structure including nested properties like Allowed and Used license counts.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $license = Get-OrchLicense PS Orch1:\> $usage = ($license.Used.Unattended / $license.Allowed.Unattended) * 100 PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Unattended license usage: $usage%"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Calculates and displays the percentage of unattended licenses currently in use.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $license = Get-OrchLicense PS Orch1:\> $expiryDate = [DateTimeOffset]::FromUnixTimeSeconds($license.ExpireDate) PS Orch1:\> $daysLeft = ($expiryDate - [DateTimeOffset]::Now).Days PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "License expires in $daysLeft days"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Calculates and displays the number of days remaining until license expiration.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets named user license information from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser cmdlet retrieves named user license information from UiPath Orchestrator. Named user licenses are assigned to specific users and allow them to use UiPath robots for different robot types (Attended, StudioPro, Unattended, NonProduction, TestAutomation, etc.).
Each returned object contains information about user licensing status, machine assignments, login history, and license consumption details for different robot types.
This is a tenant-level operation that retrieves license information across the entire Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/LicensesNamedUser/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesNamedUser(robotType='{robotType}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.License or OR.License.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - License.View]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve named user license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Attended", "StudioPro", "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", and others.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve named user license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Attended", "StudioPro", "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", and others.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Named user licenses are assigned to specific users for different robot types
- The -RobotType parameter supports wildcard characters for flexible filtering
- Common robot types include "Attended", "StudioPro", "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", and others
- Key properties include IsLicensed (license status), MachinesCount (assigned machines), and LastLoginDate (usage tracking)
- MachineNames array contains all assigned machines, while ActiveMachineNames contains currently active machines
- IsExternalLicensed indicates if the license is managed externally
- This cmdlet operates at the tenant level and does not require navigation to specific folders
- Use filtering operations to analyze license usage patterns and identify inactive or over-allocated users
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/LicensesNamedUser/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesNamedUser OAuth required scopes: OR.Licenses or OR.Licenses.Read Required permissions: Licenses.View

maml:description.maml:para: Gets runtime license information for robots from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchLicenseRuntime cmdlet retrieves runtime license information for robots from UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet provides details about robot licensing status, including whether robots are licensed, enabled, online status, and runtime capacity for different robot types (Unattended, NonProduction, TestAutomation, etc.).
Each returned object contains comprehensive information about robot machines, including machine details, license status, runtime counts, and current execution state.
This is a tenant-level operation that retrieves license information across the entire Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/LicensesRuntime/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesRuntime(robotType='{robotType}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.License or OR.License.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - License.View]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve runtime license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", "Development", and others.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve runtime license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", "Development", and others.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Runtime licenses control the execution capacity for different robot types
- The -RobotType parameter supports wildcard characters for flexible filtering
- Common robot types include "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", "Development", and others
- Key properties include IsLicensed (license status), IsOnline (connectivity), Runtimes (capacity), and ExecutingCount (current usage)
- MachineScope indicates whether the machine is in Default tenant scope or PersonalWorkspace
- This cmdlet operates at the tenant level and does not require navigation to specific folders
- Use filtering and grouping operations to analyze license consumption patterns across different robot types
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/LicensesRuntime/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesRuntime OAuth required scopes: OR.Licenses or OR.Licenses.Read Required permissions: Licenses.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information for all robot types from the current Orchestrator tenant, grouped by robot type.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information for both Unattended and NonProduction robot types.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 4: Get license details and examine structure -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first license record and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the licensing usage statistics from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchLicenseStats` cmdlet retrieves historical licensing usage statistics from UiPath Orchestrator. This provides valuable insights into license consumption patterns over time, helping administrators monitor usage trends, plan capacity, and ensure license compliance.

The cmdlet returns time-series data showing license usage counts by robot type (e.g., Unattended, RpaDeveloperPro) across different time periods. This data is essential for understanding licensing patterns, identifying peak usage periods, and making informed decisions about license allocation.
The statistics include daily snapshots of license usage, allowing administrators to track how different types of licenses are consumed over time and identify trends in automation activity.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetLicenseStats?tenantId={tenantId}&days={days}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: License.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies a time period for recent stats. Valid values: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies a time period for recent stats. Valid values: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - License statistics provide historical usage data to help with capacity planning and compliance monitoring
- The robotType property shows license types such as "Unattended", "RpaDeveloperPro", etc., but is stored internally as numeric values
- Data represents daily snapshots of license usage, not real-time consumption
- Statistics may not include the current day depending on data processing schedules
- The count property indicates the number of licenses of that type in use on the given date
- Use ConvertTo-Json to see the complete object structure including numeric robot type values
- Valid -Last parameter values are case-sensitive: 'Day', 'Week', 'Month', '3Months', '6Months', 'Year', '3Years'
- The timestamp is provided in UTC format
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetLicenseStats OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read Required permissions: License.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the default licensing usage statistics for the current Orchestrator instance.
Gets licensing usage statistics for the last 7 days.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last Month | Group-Object robotType | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets licensing usage statistics for the last 30 days grouped by robot type to see usage distribution.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $stats = Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last Month PS Orch1:\> $stats | Where-Object robotType -eq "Unattended" | Measure-Object count -Sum

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets statistics for the last 30 days and calculates total Unattended license usage.
Gets licensing usage statistics for the last 24 hours and converts to JSON format to see the complete object structure, including numeric robot type values.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves execution logs from UiPath Orchestrator with filtering capabilities.

The Get-OrchLog cmdlet retrieves execution logs from UiPath Orchestrator with comprehensive filtering capabilities. Logs provide detailed information about automation execution, errors, and system activities. This cmdlet requires at least one filter parameter to prevent excessive data retrieval.

Logs contain information such as Level (severity), TimeStamp, Machine, WindowsIdentity, ProcessName, JobKey, and detailed message content. The cmdlet supports various filtering options including time ranges, log levels, machines, processes, and job keys.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Use pagination parameters (Skip, First) to manage large result sets efficiently. Important : This cmdlet requires at least one filter parameter (such as TimeStampAfter, Last, Level, Machine, ProcessName, or JobKey) to execute. Without filters, it will output cached contents with a warning.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Logs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: Logs.View
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the search operation. Essential for comprehensive log discovery.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the log levels to filter. Common values include Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error, Fatal.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the earliest timestamp for filtering logs. Only logs after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest timestamp for filtering logs. Only logs before this time will be returned.
Specifies the number of log entries to skip from the beginning of the result set. Useful for pagination.
Specifies the maximum number of log entries to return from the beginning of the result set.
Specifies a time period for recent logs. Valid values: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.
Specifies the machine names to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs from specific robots or machines.
Specifies the Windows identities to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs for specific user accounts.
Specifies the process names to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs for specific automation processes.
Specifies whether to order results in ascending (true) or descending (false) order.
Specifies the field to order results by (e.g., TimeStamp, Level, Machine).
Specifies the job keys to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs for specific automation executions.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the log levels to filter. Common values include Trace, Debug, Info, Warn, Error, Fatal.
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the search operation. Essential for comprehensive log discovery.
Specifies the earliest timestamp for filtering logs. Only logs after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest timestamp for filtering logs. Only logs before this time will be returned.
Specifies the number of log entries to skip from the beginning of the result set. Useful for pagination.
Specifies the maximum number of log entries to return from the beginning of the result set.
Specifies a time period for recent logs. Valid values: Hour, Day, Week, Month, 3Months, 6Months, Year, 3Years.
Specifies the machine names to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs from specific robots or machines.
Specifies the Windows identities to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs for specific user accounts.
Specifies the process names to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs for specific automation processes.
Specifies whether to order results in ascending (true) or descending (false) order.
Specifies the field to order results by (e.g., TimeStamp, Level, Machine).
Specifies the job keys to filter logs. Use to retrieve logs for specific automation executions.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a folder entity operation requiring at least one filter parameter to prevent excessive data retrieval. The cmdlet will output cached contents with a warning if no filter parameters are specified. Use pagination parameters (Skip, First) to manage large result sets. Common filter patterns include time ranges (Last, TimeStampAfter), severity levels (Level), and specific execution contexts (Machine, ProcessName, JobKey). This operation requires Logs.View permissions in the target folders.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/RobotLogs OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read Required permissions: Logs.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first 10 logs from the last day.
Gets all error and fatal logs from the last 2 hours.
Retrieves the first 20 logs for the InvoiceProcess in the Production folder.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLog -Recurse -Last Week -Machine Robot01 -Level Warn, Error

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets warning and error logs from Robot01 for the last week across all folders in that tenant.
Retrieves logs for a specific job key ordered by timestamp in ascending order.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLog -Path Orch1:\Shared -Last Month -WindowsIdentity DOMAIN\robotuser -Skip 100 -First 50

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets logs for a specific Windows identity from the last month, skipping the first 100 results and taking the next 50.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machines from UiPath Orchestrator.

Gets machine information from UiPath Orchestrator tenants. Machines represent the compute resources where robots execute automation processes, including both physical machines and machine templates used for robot provisioning.

This cmdlet returns comprehensive machine information including machine types (Standard, Template), scopes (Default, PersonalWorkspace, Serverless, AgentService), license slot allocations, robot user assignments, and configuration details.
Machines are tenant entities that operate across the entire tenant scope. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants by drive name.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Machines?$expand=UpdateInfo
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Read
Required permissions: Machines.View
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Specifies the names of machines to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Expands robot user details for each machine, showing which users are assigned to run robots on the machines.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.

Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. The exported CSV can be used with Import-Csv and piped to New-OrchMachine for bulk operations.
Expands robot user details for each machine, showing which users are assigned to run robots on the machines.
Specifies the names of machines to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.

Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. The exported CSV can be used with Import-Csv and piped to New-OrchMachine for bulk operations.

maml:alert.maml:para: Machine entities are tenant-scoped and operate across the entire tenant.
Machine types include Standard (physical machines) and Template (machine templates for provisioning). Scopes include Default, PersonalWorkspace, Serverless, and AgentService.
Use -ExpandRobotUser when you need detailed information about which users are assigned to run robots on specific machines.
The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files with human-readable names instead of internal IDs.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Machines OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Read Required permissions: Machines.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machines from the current tenant.
Gets machines containing "Template" in their name.
Gets machines from multiple tenants.
Gets standard (non-template) machines.
Gets machines with unattended robot slots allocated.
Gets machines with expanded robot user details.
Exports all machines to CSV with UTF-8 BOM encoding.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves the creation date and time of client secrets from multiple machines.

The Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId cmdlet retrieves client secret information for machines, including creation dates and identifiers. Client secrets are used for machine authentication to Orchestrator and have expiration policies for security management.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
This cmdlet is useful for security audits, secret rotation management, and identifying secrets that need renewal based on their creation dates.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/clientsecrets/{licenseKey}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines
Required permissions: Machines.View
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the names of the machines for which client secret information should be retrieved.
Specifies the client secret IDs to be retrieved. This can be used to query specific secrets by their identifiers.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the names of the machines for which client secret information should be retrieved.
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the client secret IDs to be retrieved. This can be used to query specific secrets by their identifiers.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Primary Endpoint: GET /api/clientsecrets OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER] Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER]

dev:remarks.maml:para: Outputs the issuance date and time of client secrets for all machines in the current tenant.
Outputs the issuance date and time of client secrets for the specified machines.
Gets client secret information for all machines whose names contain "Prod".
