command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchMachineClientSecretId -Path Orch1:, Orch2: Machine01

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets client secret information for Machine01 across multiple tenants.
Gets client secret information for secrets whose IDs contain "123456".

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machine runtime sessions from UiPath Orchestrator.

Retrieves machine runtime sessions from UiPath Orchestrator, showing connection status and runtime information for machines across folders. This cmdlet provides information about machine connectivity, runtime availability, and session details including robot execution capacity.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimesByFolderId(folderId={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read
Required permissions: (Machines.View or Jobs.Create)
This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either navigation to the target folder or specification of target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for machine sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the status of the machine sessions to be retrieved. Common values include Connected, Disconnected, and others. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching.
Specifies how progress information is displayed during the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for machine sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the status of the machine sessions to be retrieved. Common values include Connected, Disconnected, and others. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching.
Specifies how progress information is displayed during the operation.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either: - Navigation to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), OR

- Specification of target folders using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth before other parameters when using multiple parameters. Machine Session Information: - SessionId: Unique identifier for the machine session
- Status: Connection status (Connected, Disconnected, etc.)
- RuntimeType: Type of runtime (Headless, AutomationCloud, etc.)
- Runtimes: Total available runtime slots
- UsedRuntimes: Currently used runtime slots
- MaintenanceMode: Current maintenance mode setting Common Status Values: - Connected: Machine is actively connected
- Disconnected: Machine is not currently connected
- Busy: Machine is actively executing jobs Use Cases: - Monitor machine connectivity across environments
- Check runtime availability and utilization
- Troubleshoot machine connection issues

- Capacity planning for robot deployment Important Note about Path Selection: When using Select-Object with folder entities, always include Path as the first property to identify which folder each entity belongs to. This is essential for managing entities across multiple folders.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete machine session object structure including detailed connection information and runtime statistics.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimesByFolderId OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read Required permissions: Robots.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine sessions in the current folder.
Gets available machine sessions from all folders in the current drive using -Recurse and -Status filter.
Gets disconnected machine sessions from the Shared folder, demonstrating -Path and -Status usage.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineSession -Recurse | Select-Object Path, MachineName, Status, Runtimes | ConvertTo-Json

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine sessions and displays key information in JSON format, combining output customization techniques.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the process packages from UiPath Orchestrator.
Process packages exist in both tenant feeds (tenant-wide scope) and folder feeds (folder-specific scope). This cmdlet operates as both a tenant entity and folder entity, depending on the context.

Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify target folders using -Path, -Recurse parameters. For tenant-wide package access, specify the tenant drive (e.g., Orch1:) in the -Path parameter.

The Get-OrchPackage cmdlet retrieves process packages from UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet allows you to list packages from tenant feeds or folder feeds, with options to filter by ID, specify target folders, and include subfolders recursively.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Processes&feedId={feedId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: (Packages.View - Lists packages in a Tenant Feed) and (FolderPackages.View - Lists packages in a Folder Feed)
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. Supports multiple folder paths.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the Id of the process packages to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters and multiple values.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the Id of the process packages to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters and multiple values.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. Supports multiple folder paths.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.

maml:alert.maml:para: - If no active connection exists, the cmdlet will automatically establish a connection to UiPath Orchestrator. For non-confidential applications, PKCE authentication (browser login) will be initiated on the first cmdlet execution.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read Required permissions: Libraries.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all process packages in the current folder context.
Gets all process packages from all folders recursively.
Gets process packages with IDs starting with "ProcessA" from the Production folder.
Gets TestProcess package from the tenant feed (entire tenant scope).

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPackage -Recurse | Select-Object Path, Id, Version, IsActive

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all packages recursively and displays key properties with Path shown first.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves package versions from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchPackageVersion cmdlet retrieves package versions from UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet is unique as it operates on both tenant entities and folder entities, allowing access to packages from both folder feeds and tenant feeds. Packages represent automation processes, libraries, and other executable components with version control and deployment management.
Packages can be deployed to two types of feeds: - Folder feeds: Packages deployed to specific folders with package feeds enabled
- Tenant feeds: Packages available at the tenant level that can be deployed to any folder

When executed from a folder with no feed, the cmdlet automatically retrieves packages from the tenant feed. When executed from a folder with a feed, it retrieves packages from that folder's feed. Use -Path to explicitly specify tenant feeds (Orch1:) or folder feeds (Orch1:\FolderName).

Package versions include comprehensive information such as Version number, Published date, IsActive status, Description, TargetFramework (Windows, Cross-platform), MainEntryPointPath, and project metadata. The cmdlet provides visibility into available automation packages and their deployment status across both folder and tenant levels.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Packages
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Packages.View
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the package IDs to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible package selection.
Specifies the Version of the process packages to be retrieved.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the package IDs to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible package selection.
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted. For tenant-level operations.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the Version of the process packages to be retrieved.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a tenant-level entity operation for accessing package version information. Packages represent automation processes and libraries with version control and deployment management. Use IsActive property to identify currently deployed versions. ProjectType distinguishes between executable processes and reusable libraries. TargetFramework indicates platform compatibility. This operation requires Packages.View permissions.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Processes/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetProcessVersions OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read Required permissions: Packages.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all package versions from the current folder feed, displaying Id, Version, Published date, IsActive status, and Description.

Retrieves all package versions from the tenant feed and all folder feeds using -Recurse. This demonstrates accessing the tenant feed (packages available tenant-wide) as opposed to folder feeds (packages specific to individual folders). The command can be executed from any location by explicitly specifying the tenant path.
Gets all package versions with IDs containing "Process" in the Orch1 tenant feed.
Retrieves only active package versions.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPackageVersion | Where-Object {$_.ProjectType -eq "Library"} | Select-Object Id, Version, Description

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays library packages with their versions and descriptions.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets personal workspaces from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace` cmdlet retrieves personal workspace information from UiPath Orchestrator. Personal workspaces are individual automation environments assigned to specific users, providing isolated spaces for development, testing, and personal automation activities.

Personal workspaces enable individual productivity by giving users dedicated environments where they can develop, test, and manage automation projects independently without affecting shared organizational resources. Each workspace is associated with a specific user and provides isolation for development activities.

This cmdlet operates on tenant entities and provides information about workspace ownership, activity status, last login times, and exploring users. Personal workspaces support various automation development scenarios including process testing, personal automation, and isolated development environments.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/PersonalWorkspaces
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: Folders.View
Specifies the tenant path(s) to search for personal workspaces. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant is used.
Specifies the name(s) of the personal workspaces to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by the cmdlet.
Specifies the name(s) of the personal workspaces to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the tenant path(s) to search for personal workspaces. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant is used.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by the cmdlet.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities and can be run from any location within an Orchestrator environment. Personal Workspace Information: - Id: Unique identifier for the workspace
- Key: Workspace key for API operations
- Name: Display name of the personal workspace
- IsActive: Whether the workspace is currently active
- OwnerId: Unique identifier of the workspace owner
- OwnerName: Display name of the workspace owner
- LastLogin: Timestamp of the last workspace access
- ExploringUserIds: Array of user IDs with exploring access Workspace States: - Active: Workspace is currently available for use
- Inactive: Workspace is disabled or suspended
- Exploring: Workspace has additional users with exploring access Common Use Cases: - Monitor personal workspace usage across the organization
- Identify inactive workspaces for cleanup or reactivation
- Track workspace ownership and access patterns
- Audit personal workspace allocation and utilization
- Generate reports on workspace activity and engagement Management Scenarios: - Capacity planning for personal workspace resources
- User onboarding and workspace provisioning tracking
- Identifying workspaces for archival or removal

- Monitoring collaborative workspace usage (exploring users) Important Note about Path Selection: When using Select-Object with the results, always include Path as the first property to identify which tenant each workspace belongs to. This is essential for managing workspaces across multiple tenants.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete workspace object structure including detailed ownership and access information.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/PersonalWorkspaces OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read Required permissions: Units.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all personal workspaces in the Orch1 tenant.
Gets personal workspaces whose names contain "workspace" using wildcard pattern matching and positional parameter.
Gets personal workspaces from both Orch1 and Orch2 tenants for cross-tenant analysis. Demonstrates that -Path parameter allows execution from any location.
Gets all personal workspaces and displays their structure in JSON format for detailed analysis of workspace properties including ownership and activity status.
Gets only active personal workspaces using pipeline filtering.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace | Select-Object Path, Name, OwnerName, IsActive, LastLogin

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets personal workspaces with selected properties, showing Path first for multi-tenant identification.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the processes.

Gets process information from Orchestrator folders. This cmdlet retrieves basic process metadata by default, with option to expand detailed information including process settings, arguments, and runtime configurations.

Processes are folder entities that operate within specific folder scopes. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Releases/?$expand=Environment,CurrentVersion,ReleaseVersions,EntryPoint
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Processes.View
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Specifies the names of processes to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. Can be used with corresponding Import cmdlets.
Retrieves detailed process information including ProcessSettings, Arguments, and VideoRecordingSettings. Without this parameter, these properties return null values.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Specifies the names of processes to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. Can be used with corresponding Import cmdlets.
Retrieves detailed process information including ProcessSettings, Arguments, and VideoRecordingSettings. Without this parameter, these properties return null values.

maml:alert.maml:para: Process entities are folder-scoped. You must navigate to a folder or use -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters to specify target folders.
Use -ExpandDetails when you need access to ProcessSettings properties such as AutopilotForRobots, VideoRecordingSettings, or detailed Arguments metadata.
The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files with human-readable names instead of internal IDs.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Releases OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read Required permissions: Processes.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets processes from the current folder.
Gets processes from the current folder and all subfolders.
Gets processes containing "Invoice" in their name from all folders.
Gets processes from specific folders and their subfolders.
Gets specific processes by name pattern from all folders.
Gets processes with Autopilot for Robots enabled using expanded details.
Gets high-priority processes from all folders.
Exports all processes to CSV with UTF-8 BOM encoding. The exported CSV can be imported using Import-Csv | New-OrchProcess or Import-Csv | Update-OrchProcess.

maml:description.maml:para: Lists all configured UiPath Orchestrator drives and their connection status.

The Get-OrchPSDrive cmdlet retrieves information about all configured UiPath Orchestrator PowerShell drives. This cmdlet is essential for environment verification and should be the first command executed in any UiPathOrch session to verify available connections and their status.
Primary Endpoint: N/A (Local configuration)
OAuth required scopes: N/A
Required permissions: N/A
Specifies drive name patterns to filter results. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet.

Forces connection to enabled drives to retrieve complete information including API version and connection status. By default, Get-OrchPSDrive only displays configuration file information for unconnected drives to avoid authentication API calls and potential browser login prompts for non-confidential app configurations.

Forces connection to enabled drives to retrieve complete information including API version and connection status. By default, Get-OrchPSDrive only displays configuration file information for unconnected drives to avoid authentication API calls and potential browser login prompts for non-confidential app configurations.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet.
Specifies drive name patterns to filter results. Supports wildcards and comma-separated values.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet should always be executed at the beginning of any UiPathOrch session to verify the environment and available connections. - By default, displays configuration information for all drives without connecting
- Connected drives show additional details: ApiVersion, CurrentUser, TenantId, AccessToken
- Unconnected drives only show configuration file information to avoid authentication overhead
- Use -Force to connect all enabled drives and retrieve complete connection details
- Non-confidential app drives may require browser login (PKCE) when using -Force

dev:remarks.maml:para: Lists all configured UiPath Orchestrator drives with their connection details.

command:example.maml:title: ---------------- Example 2: Show shorten Scope ----------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchPSDrive Orch1: | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Scope

dev:remarks.maml:para: UiPathOrch internally converts long scopes into their equivalent shorter forms. These shortened scopes are not automatically written back to the configuration file, but you can check them using the command above.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 3: Display all information related to the drives ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: You can check tenant-related information such as `PartitionGlobalId`, `TenantId`, `TenantKey`, and `AccessToken`. Note that this information is displayed only for tenants with an established connection. To force a connection, use the `-Force` switch parameter.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the queues.

Gets queue information from UiPath Orchestrator folders. Queues are data repositories that enable robots to exchange information and work items in an organized manner, supporting both attended and unattended automation scenarios.

This cmdlet returns queue information including definitions, retry policies, encryption settings, SLA configurations, retention policies, and organizational unit assignments. It operates on folder entities and supports recursive retrieval across folder hierarchies.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueDefinitions
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Read
Required permissions: Queues.View
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Specifies the names of queues to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. Can be used with corresponding Import cmdlets.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Specifies the names of queues to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. Can be used with corresponding Import cmdlets.

maml:alert.maml:para: Queue entities are folder-scoped. You must navigate to a folder or use -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters to specify target folders.
Queues support various configurations including retry policies, encryption, SLA settings, and retention policies for managing automation workflows and data processing.
The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files with human-readable names instead of internal IDs.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueDefinitions OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Read Required permissions: Queues.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets queues from the current folder.
Gets queues from all folders recursively.
Gets queues containing "Invoice" in their name from all folders.
Gets queues from specific folders and their subfolders.
Gets encrypted queues from all folders.
Gets queues with more than 3 retry attempts configured.
Exports all queues to CSV with UTF-8 BOM encoding. The exported CSV can be imported using New-OrchQueue and Update-OrchQueue cmdlets.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves queue items from UiPath Orchestrator queues with comprehensive filtering capabilities.

The Get-OrchQueueItem cmdlet retrieves queue items from UiPath Orchestrator queues with comprehensive filtering capabilities. Queue items represent work units in automation processes, containing data to be processed and tracking their execution status, priority, and processing history.

Each queue item contains extensive information including Status (New, InProgress, Successful, Failed, etc.), Priority (High, Normal, Low), processing timestamps, robot assignment, specific content data, exception details, and review information. Items also include unique identifiers (Id, Key, UniqueKey) and processing metadata.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Important : This cmdlet requires at least one filter parameter to prevent excessive data retrieval. Without filters, it will output cached contents with a warning.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueItems
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Read
Required permissions: Queues.View
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the search operation. Essential for comprehensive queue item discovery.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the names of queues whose items should be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible queue selection.
Specifies the latest end processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that finished processing before this time will be returned.
Specifies the earliest end processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that finished processing after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest due date for filtering queue items. Only items with due dates before this time will be returned.
Specifies the earliest due date for filtering queue items. Only items with due dates after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest defer date for filtering queue items. Only items with defer dates before this time will be returned.
Specifies the earliest defer date for filtering queue items. Only items with defer dates after this time will be returned.
Specifies the field to order results by (e.g., StartProcessing, EndProcessing, Priority, Status).
Specifies whether to order results in ascending (true) or descending (false) order.
Specifies the robot names to filter queue items. Use to find items processed by specific robots.
Specifies the earliest start processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that started processing after this time will be returned.
Specifies the revision numbers to filter queue items.
Specifies the reviewer usernames to filter queue items. Use to find items assigned to specific reviewers.
Specifies the priority levels to filter queue items. Common values include High, Normal, Low.
Specifies the exception types to filter queue items. Use to find items with specific error types.
Specifies the maximum number of queue items to return from the beginning of the result set.
Specifies the number of queue items to skip from the beginning of the result set. Useful for pagination.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the status values to filter queue items. Common values include New, InProgress, Successful, Failed, Abandoned, Retried, Deleted.
Specifies the latest start processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that started processing before this time will be returned.
Specifies the names of queues whose items should be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible queue selection.
Specifies the target folders to search. If not specified, the current folder context will be used. For folder entity operations requiring path specification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the number of queue items to skip from the beginning of the result set. Useful for pagination.
Specifies the maximum number of queue items to return from the beginning of the result set.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the search operation. Essential for comprehensive queue item discovery.
Specifies the exception types to filter queue items. Use to find items with specific error types.
Specifies the priority levels to filter queue items. Common values include High, Normal, Low.
Specifies the reviewer usernames to filter queue items. Use to find items assigned to specific reviewers.
Specifies the revision numbers to filter queue items.
Specifies the robot names to filter queue items. Use to find items processed by specific robots.
Specifies the status values to filter queue items. Common values include New, InProgress, Successful, Failed, Abandoned, Retried, Deleted.
Specifies whether to order results in ascending (true) or descending (false) order.
Specifies the field to order results by (e.g., StartProcessing, EndProcessing, Priority, Status).
Specifies the earliest defer date for filtering queue items. Only items with defer dates after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest defer date for filtering queue items. Only items with defer dates before this time will be returned.
Specifies the earliest due date for filtering queue items. Only items with due dates after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest due date for filtering queue items. Only items with due dates before this time will be returned.
Specifies the earliest end processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that finished processing after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest end processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that finished processing before this time will be returned.
Specifies the earliest start processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that started processing after this time will be returned.
Specifies the latest start processing time for filtering queue items. Only items that started processing before this time will be returned.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a folder entity operation requiring at least one filter parameter to prevent excessive data retrieval. The cmdlet will output cached contents with a warning if no filter parameters are specified. Common filter patterns include queue names (Name), status values (Status), time ranges (StartProcessingAfter/Before), and robot assignments (Robot). Use pagination parameters (Skip, First) to manage large result sets. This operation requires Queues.View permissions in the target folders.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueItems OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Read Required permissions: Transactions.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first 5 queue items with New status from the current folder.
Gets the first 10 failed queue items from the ProcessingQueue.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchQueueItem -Path Orch1:\Production -Recurse -Status New, InProgress -Priority High

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves high-priority queue items with New or InProgress status from the Production folder.
Gets queue items that started processing in the last 2 hours and displays detailed JSON information including SpecificContent and ProcessingException.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchQueueItem -Path Orch1:\Production -Robot Robot01 -Status Successful -OrderBy EndProcessing -OrderAscending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves successful queue items processed by Robot01 from the Production folder, ordered by end processing time in ascending order (oldest first).
Gets queue items from ReviewQueue assigned to reviewer john.doe, skipping the first 20 and taking the next 10.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets robots autoprovisioned from users.

OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read
Required permissions: Users.View and Robots.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
Specifies the UserName of the robots to be retrieved.
Specifies the FullName of the robots to be retrieved.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the roles.

Gets role information from UiPath Orchestrator tenants. Roles define permissions and access rights for users and can be either tenant-level or folder-level. This cmdlet retrieves role metadata and can expand detailed permission information.
Roles are tenant entities that operate across the entire tenant scope. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants by drive name.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Roles?$expand=Permissions
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: Roles.View or Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Expands detailed permission information for each role, showing individual permissions like Assets.View, Processes.Edit, etc.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the names of roles to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. Can be used with corresponding Import cmdlets.
Expands detailed permission information for each role, showing individual permissions like Assets.View, Processes.Edit, etc.
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the names of roles to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.
Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. Can be used with corresponding Import cmdlets.

maml:alert.maml:para: Role entities are tenant-scoped. They operate across the entire tenant and are not folder-specific.
Roles can be either Tenant or Folder type. Static roles are built-in and cannot be modified, while custom roles can be edited.
Use -ExpandPermission when you need detailed permission analysis or want to filter roles by specific permissions.
The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files with human-readable names instead of internal IDs.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Roles OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read Required permissions: Roles.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all roles from the current tenant.
Gets roles containing "Admin" in their name.
Gets roles from multiple tenants.
Gets roles with Assets permission using expanded details.
Gets custom (non-static) roles that can be modified.
Exports all roles to CSV with UTF-8 BOM encoding. The exported CSV can be imported using Import-Csv | Set-OrchRole.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchSetting` cmdlet retrieves configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes various system settings such as localization, email configuration, authentication settings, deployment settings, feature toggles, and other operational parameters.
Settings control various aspects of Orchestrator behavior including mail server configuration, timezone settings, authentication policies, trigger settings, and feature availability.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the folder path(s) to search for settings. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths. If not specified, the current location is used.
Specifies the name(s) of the settings to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all settings are returned.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name(s) of the settings to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all settings are returned.
Specifies the folder path(s) to search for settings. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths. If not specified, the current location is used.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Settings include system-wide configuration for various Orchestrator features

- Common setting categories include: Localization (Abp.Localization. ), Email (Abp.Net.Mail. ), Authentication (Auth. ), Deployment (Deployment. ), Features (Features. ), and Triggers (Triggers. ) - Some setting values may be masked for security (shown as ••••••••••••)
- The Scope property indicates the scope level of the setting
- Settings control fundamental Orchestrator behavior and should be modified carefully
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all configuration settings from the current Orchestrator instance.
Gets settings related to language and timezone using wildcard patterns.
Gets authentication-related settings from multiple tenants.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchSetting *Email* | Select-Object Path, Name, Value

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets email-related settings and displays key properties with Path shown first.
Gets trigger-related settings and outputs detailed structure in JSON format.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test case definitions from the Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchTestCase cmdlet retrieves test case definitions from the Orchestrator environment. Test cases are workflow files (.xaml) that have been designated as test cases within automation packages and can be executed as part of test sets for automated testing scenarios.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive traversal to find test cases across different folder hierarchies. Test cases are associated with specific packages and versions, enabling version-controlled testing and quality assurance processes.

Test case definitions include metadata about the associated package, version information, creation and modification details, and feed associations. These test cases can be organized into test sets and executed to validate automation workflows.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/TestCaseDefinitions
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSets or OR.TestSets.Read
Required permissions: TestSets.View

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test case definitions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Recursively gets test case definitions from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the names of the test case definitions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test case definitions will be returned.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the names of the test case definitions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test case definitions will be returned.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test case definitions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Recursively gets test case definitions from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
Test case definitions are associated with automation packages and contain important version information: - PackageIdentifier: The automation package containing the test case
- AppVersion: The application version of the test case
- CreatedVersion: The package version when the test case was created
- LatestVersion: The most recent version of the associated package
- FeedId: The package feed association
Parameter placement is important for folder entity cmdlets. Place -Path, -Recurse, -Depth parameters immediately after the cmdlet name for proper auto-completion functionality.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of test case definition objects, as they contain detailed metadata including version tracking and package associations.
Test case definitions serve as the foundation for automated testing workflows and can be organized into test sets for comprehensive quality assurance processes.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/TestCaseDefinitions OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSets or OR.TestSets.Read Required permissions: TestSets.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all test case definitions from the current folder.
Retrieves all test case definitions whose names contain "Test" using wildcard matching.
Retrieves all test case definitions from the current folder and all subfolders recursively.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test case execution records from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchTestCaseExecution cmdlet retrieves test case execution records from UiPath Orchestrator. Test case executions represent individual runs of automated test cases, including their execution status, timing information, input/output arguments, and related metadata.
This is a folder entity operation. You must either navigate to a specific folder using Set-Location (cd) or use the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters to specify target folders.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves test data queues from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchTestDataQueue cmdlet retrieves test data queues from UiPath Orchestrator. Test data queues provide structured test data management for automation testing, enabling organized storage and retrieval of test datasets used in automation validation and quality assurance processes.

Test data queues contain structured data sets that can be consumed by test automation workflows, supporting data-driven testing scenarios. These queues enable separation of test data from test logic, facilitating maintainable and scalable test automation practices.

This cmdlet operates as a folder entity operation, requiring navigation to the appropriate folder context or specification of target folders using the -Path parameter. Use the -Recurse parameter to include test data queues from subfolders, and -Depth to control recursion levels.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/TestDataQueues
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER]

Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the search operation. Essential for comprehensive test data queue discovery.
Specifies the depth for recursion into target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only. Higher values include more subfolder levels.
Specifies the names of test data queues to be retrieved. Supports wildcard patterns for flexible queue selection.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test data queue items in expanded table format from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchTestDataQueueItemTable` cmdlet retrieves test data queue items from UiPath Orchestrator and displays them in an expanded table format. This cmdlet is implemented as a function that transforms the internal JSON format of test data into a readable table structure.

While `Get-OrchTestDataQueueItem` returns test data in internal JSON format that cannot be displayed as a table, this cmdlet specifically addresses that limitation by expanding variable-format test data into a structured table presentation. This makes it ideal for viewing, analyzing, and reporting on test data content.

This is a folder entity operation cmdlet that requires either navigating to a specific folder containing test data queues or using the -Path or -Recurse parameters to specify target folders. The cmdlet dynamically adapts to the schema of the test data, displaying all columns and values in a user-friendly table format.

The cmdlet is particularly useful for data-driven testing scenarios where test data has varying column structures, as it can handle and display any test data schema by expanding the JSON content into appropriate table columns.
When specifying the -Path and -Recurse parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: Function implementation (uses Get-OrchTestDataQueueItem internally which calls GET /odata/TestDataQueueItems)
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestData.Read
Required permissions: Folders.View, TestData.View (for test data queue item access)
Specifies the folder path containing test data queues. If not specified, the current location is used. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.
Includes test data queue items from subfolders when retrieving items. This is a folder entity operation parameter that allows scanning multiple folders for test data.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the folder path containing test data queues. If not specified, the current location is used. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.
Includes test data queue items from subfolders when retrieving items. This is a folder entity operation parameter that allows scanning multiple folders for test data.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This is a folder entity operation cmdlet that requires navigation to a test data folder or use of -Path/-Recurse parameters
- This cmdlet is implemented as a function that transforms internal JSON test data into table format
- Unlike Get-OrchTestDataQueueItem which returns JSON format, this cmdlet provides user-friendly table output
- The table format dynamically adapts to the test data schema, displaying all available columns
- The cmdlet handles variable-format test data by expanding JSON content into appropriate table columns
- Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure including table formatting metadata
- The IsConsumed property indicates whether test data items have been used in test execution
- This cmdlet is essential for data-driven testing scenarios where readable test data presentation is needed

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the test set execution.

The Get-OrchTestSetExecution cmdlet retrieves test set execution records from UiPath Orchestrator. Test set executions represent the historical records of test set runs, including their status, execution time, and results.
This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.
You can filter executions by status, time range, trigger type, and other criteria. The cmdlet supports pagination through -Skip and -First parameters for large result sets.

If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no trigger names are specified, it outputs all triggers in the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets unattended robot sessions from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchUnattendedSession cmdlet retrieves information about unattended robot sessions from UiPath Orchestrator. Unattended sessions represent robot runtime environments that can execute processes without human intervention. Each session provides details about the robot machine, runtime type, status, and configuration.
This is a tenant-level operation that retrieves session information across the entire Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimes
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read
Required permissions: Robots.View

Specifies the folder paths where to search for unattended sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.

Specifies the status of the unattended sessions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common status values include "Connected", "Disconnected", "Busy", and "Unknown".
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for unattended sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.

Specifies the status of the unattended sessions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common status values include "Connected", "Disconnected", "Busy", and "Unknown".
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
Unattended sessions represent active automation sessions running on unattended robots. This information is valuable for: - Monitoring robot utilization and performance
- Identifying bottlenecks and resource conflicts
- Capacity planning and scaling decisions
- Troubleshooting automation execution issues
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of session objects, as they contain detailed runtime information that may not be visible in the default display format.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimes OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read Required permissions: Robots.View

Retrieves unattended sessions from multiple specified Orchestrator tenants for cross-tenant analysis.
Retrieves unattended sessions and displays their structure in JSON format for detailed analysis of robot session properties.
Gets only headless runtime unattended sessions using pipeline filtering.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the update settings from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchUpdateSetting cmdlet retrieves the update configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes the update server source, update server URL, and polling interval settings that control how Orchestrator checks for and manages updates.
This is a tenant-scoped entity. The -Path parameter specifies the drive name (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:) to target specific Orchestrator tenants.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetUpdateSettings
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the drive names to target specific Orchestrator tenants. Supports comma-separated values. Autocomplete available with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the drive names to target specific Orchestrator tenants. Supports comma-separated values. Autocomplete available with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - UpdateServerSource indicates the type of update source (typically "Orchestrator")
- UpdateServerUrl contains the full URL of the update server
- PollingInterval specifies the frequency (in minutes) for checking updates
- These settings control the automatic update checking behavior
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetUpdateSettings OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 1: Get update settings for current tenant ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the update settings from multiple Orchestrator tenants.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 3: Display specific properties ------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchUpdateSetting -Path Orch1: | Select-Object Path, UpdateServerSource, PollingInterval

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the update settings and displays only the key configuration properties.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets update settings from multiple tenants and converts to JSON for detailed analysis.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets users from UiPath Orchestrator.
Gets user information from UiPath Orchestrator tenants. This includes individual users, groups, and robot accounts that can authenticate and interact with the Orchestrator environment.

By default, this cmdlet uses the list API endpoint which provides basic user information efficiently. When the -ExpandDetails parameter is specified, the cmdlet calls individual user detail APIs to retrieve comprehensive information including detailed notification preferences, session permissions, and robot provisioning settings.
Users are tenant entities that operate across the entire tenant scope. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants by drive name.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: Users.View
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Specifies the usernames to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies the full names to filter by. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.

Retrieves additional detailed user information including login providers history, tenant details, and account IDs. Without this parameter, properties like LoginProviders array, TenancyName, and AccountId return empty values.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.

Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. The exported CSV can be used with Import-Csv and piped to Add-OrchUser for bulk operations.
Specifies the user types to filter by. Valid values include: DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, User, Robot.
Specifies the full names to filter by. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Specifies the usernames to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values.

Retrieves additional detailed user information including login providers history, tenant details, and account IDs. Without this parameter, properties like LoginProviders array, TenancyName, and AccountId return empty values.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export. Default is UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.

Exports results to CSV file with UTF-8 BOM encoding. Automatically converts internal IDs to human-readable names. The exported CSV can be used with Import-Csv and piped to Add-OrchUser for bulk operations.
Specifies the user types to filter by. Valid values include: DirectoryUser, DirectoryGroup, User, Robot.

maml:alert.maml:para: User entities are tenant-scoped and operate across the entire tenant.

Use -ExpandDetails when you need access to additional user information such as login providers history, tenant details (TenancyName), and account IDs. Basic user information including roles, notification preferences, and robot provisioning is available without -ExpandDetails, but some properties like LoginProviders array and AccountId require expansion.
The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files with human-readable names instead of internal IDs.
User types include DirectoryUser (individual users), DirectoryGroup (user groups), User (local users), and Robot (robot accounts).
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read Required permissions: Users.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all users from the current tenant.
Gets users containing "admin" in their username.
Gets only directory users (excluding groups and robot accounts).
Gets inactive users using expanded details.
Gets users from multiple tenants.
Gets directory users whose full name starts with "John".
Gets detailed user information and displays the complete structure including nested properties like UserRoles.
Exports all users to CSV with UTF-8 BOM encoding. The exported CSV can be imported using Import-Csv | Add-OrchUser or Import-Csv | Update-OrchUser.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves user privileges based on the union of privileges model.

The Get-OrchUserPrivilege cmdlet retrieves comprehensive user privilege information from UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet provides detailed insights into user permissions, role assignments, access levels, and session privileges across the Orchestrator environment.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.

The cmdlet returns extensive privilege information including explicit and inherited roles, access permissions, attended session privileges, personal workspace permissions, and update policies. Each privilege category shows explicit assignments, inherited permissions from group memberships, and the effective (final) permissions that apply to the user.
This information is essential for understanding the complete permission landscape for users, troubleshooting access issues, auditing user privileges, and ensuring proper security configuration.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Users/GetPrivileges?userId={userId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: Users.View
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the user names for which privilege information should be retrieved.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the user names for which privilege information should be retrieved.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Users/GetPrivileges OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read Required permissions: Users.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets privilege information for all users in the current tenant.
Gets privilege information for the user "john.doe" in the current tenant.
Gets privilege information for all users whose names contain "admin".
Gets privilege information for the "administrator" user across multiple tenants.
Gets all users who have explicit role assignments (not just inherited from groups).
Gets all user privileges and displays username, access level, and total effective role count.
Gets privilege information for admin users via pipeline and exports detailed privilege structure to JSON.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets user session information.

The Get-OrchUserSession cmdlet retrieves active and historical user session information from UiPath Orchestrator. User sessions represent interactive connections between users and Orchestrator, including login times, session duration, and connection details.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.

Session information is useful for monitoring user activity, security auditing, license usage tracking, and troubleshooting connection issues. The cmdlet provides insights into user access patterns and system utilization.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/UserSessions
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: Users.View
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the item to sort the sessions retrieved by.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the State of the sessions to be retrieved.
Specifies the Type of the sessions to be retrieved.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the item to sort the sessions retrieved by.
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the State of the sessions to be retrieved.
Specifies the Type of the sessions to be retrieved.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.

maml:alert.maml:para: Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetGlobalSessions OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read Required permissions: Robots.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets session information for all users in the current tenant.
Gets session information for the user "john.doe".
Gets session information for all users whose names contain "admin".
Gets session information for the "developer" user across multiple tenants.
Gets all currently active user sessions.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-OrchUserSession | Select-Object UserName, LoginTime, LastActivity, IsActive, SessionDuration

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all user sessions and displays key timing and status information.
Gets session information for all users who have been active within the last hour. User information is passed via pipeline using ByPropertyName binding.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets webhooks from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchWebhook` cmdlet retrieves webhook information from UiPath Orchestrator. Webhooks are HTTP callbacks that allow Orchestrator to send real-time notifications to external systems when specific events occur, such as job completions, process deployments, or queue item updates.

This cmdlet returns detailed information about configured webhooks including their URLs, event subscriptions, security settings, and operational status. It supports filtering by webhook name using wildcards.
Webhooks enable integration with external monitoring systems, notification platforms, and custom applications by providing real-time event notifications from Orchestrator.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Webhooks
OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks or OR.Webhooks.Read
Required permissions: Webhooks.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies the name(s) of the webhooks to be retrieved. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all webhooks are returned.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name(s) of the webhooks to be retrieved. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all webhooks are returned.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Webhooks provide real-time event notifications from Orchestrator to external systems
- The Events property contains an array of WebhookEvent objects specifying which events trigger the webhook
- Common event types include: job.created, job.faulted, job.completed, process.deleted, queue.created, queue.deleted, and many others
- The SubscribeToAllEvents property indicates whether the webhook receives all available event types
- The AllowInsecureSsl property controls whether the webhook accepts invalid or self-signed SSL certificates
- The Secret property (if configured) is used for webhook signature verification but is not displayed for security
- The Key property contains a unique identifier for the webhook
- Disabled webhooks (Enabled = false) do not send notifications even if events occur
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Webhooks OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks or OR.Webhooks.Read Required permissions: Webhooks.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all webhooks configured in the current Orchestrator instance.
Gets webhooks whose names start with "my" using wildcard pattern matching.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchWebhook -Path Orch1:, Orch2: integration*

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets webhooks with names starting with "integration" from multiple tenants.
Gets a specific webhook and displays the complete structure including nested properties like Events array.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets web configuration settings.

The Get-OrchWebSetting cmdlet retrieves web configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. Web settings control various aspects of the web interface, user experience, security policies, and system behavior.
This is a tenant entity cmdlet. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.

Web settings include authentication configurations, user interface preferences, security policies, feature toggles, and integration settings. These settings affect how users interact with Orchestrator and control system-wide behaviors.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/WebSettings
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
Specifies the Key of the settings to be retrieved.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the Key of the settings to be retrieved.
Specifies the name of the target tenants using drive names. If not specified, the current tenant will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetWebSettings OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read Required permissions: Settings.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all web settings in the current tenant.
Gets the web setting named "Authentication".
Gets all web settings whose names contain "Auth".
Gets the "SessionTimeout" web setting across multiple tenants.
Gets all web settings in the Security category.
