command:example.dev:code: Get-OrchWebSetting | Select-Object Name, Value, Category, Description | Format-Table

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all web settings and displays them in a formatted table with key properties.
Gets all web settings that have been modified from their default values and exports them to JSON format.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets audit log entries from Platform Management.

The Get-PmAuditLog cmdlet retrieves audit log entries from UiPath Platform Management. This cmdlet operates at the organization level and tracks administrative activities, user actions, and system events across the organization.

This is an organization entity cmdlet that calls the Platform Management API. It operates at the organization level, where multiple tenants can belong to the same organization. The -Path parameter specifies target tenants using drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:), but the audit data reflects organization-wide activities.

Platform Management audit logs provide comprehensive tracking of user management activities, role assignments, license changes, and administrative actions performed across all tenants within the organization. This is essential for compliance, security auditing, and administrative oversight.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/auditLog/{partitionGlobalId}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Administration or OR.Administration.Read
Required permissions: Administration.View
Specifies the name of the target tenant drives. The audit log data is organization-wide regardless of which tenant drive is used.
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the sorting order for createdOn. By default, entries are returned in descending order (newest first).
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Gets only the specified number of objects. Enter the number of objects to get.
Specifies the sorting order for createdOn. By default, entries are returned in descending order (newest first).
Specifies the name of the target tenant drives. The audit log data is organization-wide regardless of which tenant drive is used.
Ignores the specified number of objects and then gets the remaining objects. Enter the number of objects to skip.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets audit log entries from the current organization.
Gets the first 100 audit log entries (most recent by default).
Gets the first 50 audit log entries in ascending order (oldest first).
Gets audit log entries from the organization, accessed through multiple tenant drives.
Skips the first 100 entries and gets the next 50 audit log entries (pagination).
Gets all audit log entries for user creation events.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Get-PmAuditLog -First 1000 | Select-Object Timestamp, UserName, EventType, Details | Export-Csv "AuditLog.csv"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the first 1000 audit log entries and exports key information to a CSV file for analysis.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets external API resources from Platform Management.

The `Get-PmExternalApiResource` cmdlet retrieves external API resource information from UiPath Platform Management. External API resources define the available APIs and their associated OAuth scopes that can be accessed by external applications for integration with UiPath services.

This cmdlet returns comprehensive information about API resources including their names, display names, descriptions, and detailed scope definitions. Each API resource contains multiple scopes that define specific permissions for read and write access to different functional areas.

The scope information is essential for configuring OAuth applications that need to integrate with UiPath services, as it defines the exact permissions required for accessing specific API endpoints and functionality.
This is a Platform Management API cmdlet that works on any UiPathOrch drive (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/ExternalApiResource
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - External API resources view permissions]
Specifies the target drive path(s). This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards. Since this is a Platform Management API cmdlet, it works on any UiPathOrch drive.

Specifies the name(s) of the external API resources to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all API resources are returned.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

Specifies the name(s) of the external API resources to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all API resources are returned.
Specifies the target drive path(s). This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards. Since this is a Platform Management API cmdlet, it works on any UiPathOrch drive.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet uses Platform Management API and works on any UiPathOrch drive
- API resources include major UiPath services: Orchestrator, Test Manager, Document Understanding, AI Center, Data Service, and others
- The scopes property contains detailed OAuth scope information essential for API integration
- Common Orchestrator scopes follow the pattern OR.{Area} and OR.{Area}.Read/Write (e.g., OR.Assets, OR.Assets.Read, OR.Assets.Write)
- Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete scope structure and understand available permissions
- The information is crucial for configuring external applications that integrate with UiPath services via OAuth

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\ PS Orch1:\> Get-PmExternalApiResource

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all external API resources available in Platform Management.
Gets API resources whose names contain "Orchestrator".

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $orchApi = Get-PmExternalApiResource UiPath.Orchestrator PS Orch1:\> $orchApi.scopes | Select-Object name, description | Format-Table

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Orchestrator API resource and displays all its available OAuth scopes in a formatted table.
Gets API resources that have more than 10 scopes available.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $orchApi = Get-PmExternalApiResource UiPath.Orchestrator PS Orch1:\> $orchApi | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Orchestrator API resource and displays its complete structure in JSON format to explore all scopes and properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets external applications from Platform Management.

The `Get-PmExternalApplication` cmdlet retrieves external application information from UiPath Platform Management. External applications are OAuth applications registered for authentication and authorization with UiPath services.
This cmdlet returns details about registered applications including their names, IDs, and confidentiality settings. It supports filtering by application name using wildcards.
This is a Platform Management API cmdlet that works on any UiPathOrch drive (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/ExternalClient/{partitionGlobalId}
OAuth required scopes: PM.ExternalApplication or PM.ExternalApplication.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - External applications view permissions]
Specifies the target drive path(s). This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards. Since this is a Platform Management API cmdlet, it works on any UiPathOrch drive.
Specifies the name(s) of the external applications to be retrieved. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name(s) of the external applications to be retrieved. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the target drive path(s). This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards. Since this is a Platform Management API cmdlet, it works on any UiPathOrch drive.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet uses Platform Management API and works on any UiPathOrch drive
- The isConfidential property indicates whether the application is configured as confidential or non-confidential
- Confidential applications are typically used for server-to-server authentication
- Non-confidential applications are used for user-interactive authentication scenarios

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\ PS Orch1:\> Get-PmExternalApplication

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all external applications registered in Platform Management.
Gets external applications whose names contain "hoge" using wildcard pattern matching.
Gets only confidential external applications.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-PmExternalApplication | Select-Object name, id, isConfidential | Format-Table

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all external applications and displays them in a formatted table.
Gets specific external applications by name.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets groups from UiPath Platform Management.

The Get-PmGroup cmdlet retrieves group information from UiPath Platform Management. Groups are collections of users that can be assigned roles and permissions collectively. This cmdlet provides details about group membership, creation time, license assignments, and other group-related metadata.
The -Path parameter specifies target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath Orchestrator drives (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Group/{tenantId}
OAuth required scopes: PM.Group or PM.Group.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Platform Management group read permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies the names of groups to retrieve. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Multiple group names can be specified as an array.
Specifies the encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. This parameter controls the character encoding of the output file.

Specifies the file path to export group data as CSV. The exported CSV contains columns that match the parameter names of New-PmGroup, making it suitable for bulk import operations. This is more reliable than using Select-Object + Export-Csv as it includes proper ID-to-name conversions.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. This parameter controls the character encoding of the output file.

Specifies the file path to export group data as CSV. The exported CSV contains columns that match the parameter names of New-PmGroup, making it suitable for bulk import operations. This is more reliable than using Select-Object + Export-Csv as it includes proper ID-to-name conversions.
Specifies the names of groups to retrieve. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Multiple group names can be specified as an array.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath drive types
- The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files with proper column names matching New-PmGroup parameters
- For bulk operations, using -ExportCsv followed by Import-Csv | New-PmGroup is more reliable than Select-Object + Export-Csv
- For copying groups between folders or tenants, consider using Copy-PmGroup instead of CSV export/import for better results
- The members array contains detailed information about group membership
- Platform Management cmdlets (starting with "Pm") provide cross-organizational group information
- Group types and mappingRole properties provide information about group purpose and role assignments

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all groups from the current Platform Management instance.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Get groups from specific drives ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves group information from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 3: Get multiple groups by name ------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves information for multiple specified groups.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 4: Get group details and examine structure ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first group and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 5: Export groups to CSV for import ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports all groups to a CSV file that can be used with Import-Csv and New-PmGroup for bulk group creation.
Imports group definitions from a CSV file exported by Get-PmGroup -ExportCsv and creates new groups.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 7: Filter groups by creation date ----------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-PmGroup | Where-Object {$_.creationTime -gt (Get-Date).AddDays(-30)} | Select-Object displayName, creationTime

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all groups and filters for those created in the last 30 days, displaying name and creation time.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets group members from UiPath Platform Management.

The Get-PmGroupMember cmdlet retrieves group membership information from UiPath Platform Management. This cmdlet returns details about all users, robot accounts, and other directory entities that are members of specified groups. It supports filtering by group name and can export results to CSV format for bulk operations.
The -Path parameter specifies target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath Orchestrator drives (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Group/{tenantId}/{groupId}
OAuth required scopes: PM.Group or PM.Group.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Platform Management group read permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies the names of groups for which to retrieve members. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. If not specified, returns members from all groups.
Specifies the encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. This parameter controls the character encoding of the output file.

Specifies the file path to export group member data as CSV. The exported CSV contains columns suitable for bulk import operations with Add-PmGroupMember. This is more reliable than using Select-Object + Export-Csv as it includes proper formatting for group membership operations.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. This parameter controls the character encoding of the output file.

Specifies the file path to export group member data as CSV. The exported CSV contains columns suitable for bulk import operations with Add-PmGroupMember. This is more reliable than using Select-Object + Export-Csv as it includes proper formatting for group membership operations.
Specifies the names of groups for which to retrieve members. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. If not specified, returns members from all groups.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Group members can include users, robot accounts, nested groups, and applications
- The -ExportCsv parameter creates import-ready CSV files for bulk group membership operations
- Member types are indicated by the objectType property (DirectoryUser, DirectoryRobotUser, DirectoryGroup, DirectoryApplication)
- Use filtering operations to analyze group membership patterns and identify security compliance issues
- Platform Management cmdlets (starting with "Pm") provide cross-organizational group membership information
- The source property indicates the directory source (e.g., "local", "aad" for Azure Active Directory)

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves members from all groups in the current Platform Management instance.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 2: Get members of specific group -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves members from groups whose names start with "Admin" or are exactly "Everyone".

command:example.maml:title: -- Example 3: Get group member details and examine structure --
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-PmGroupMember Administrators | Select-Object -First 1 | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first group member and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 4: Export group members to CSV ------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Exports all members of the Administrators group to a CSV file for bulk operations or reporting. The exported CSV can be imported using Import-Csv | Add-PmGroupMember.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 5: Get group members from specific drives ------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-PmGroupMember -Path Orch1:, Orch2: Administrators

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves group membership information from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets licensed groups from Platform Management.

The Get-PmLicensedGroup cmdlet retrieves licensed group information from UiPath Platform Management. This cmdlet operates at the organization level and manages license allocations for user groups across the organization.

This is an organization entity cmdlet that calls the Platform Management API. It operates at the organization level, where multiple tenants can belong to the same organization. Licensed groups define how licenses are distributed and allocated to different user groups within the organization.

Platform Management licensed groups provide centralized license management, enabling administrators to allocate, track, and manage licenses across all tenants within the organization. This ensures efficient license utilization and proper compliance management.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/licensedgroups
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]

maml:description.maml:para: Gets licensed users from UiPath Platform Management.

The Get-PmLicensedUser cmdlet retrieves information about licensed users from UiPath Platform Management. This cmdlet provides details about users who have been assigned UiPath licenses, including their contact information, license types, and usage history.
Each returned object contains user identification, license bundle information, last usage timestamp, and orphan status (indicating whether the user account still exists in the system).
This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath Orchestrator drives (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: GET /portal_/api/license/accountant/UserLicense/user/page
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies the name(s) of the licensed users to retrieve. This parameter filters users based on their name field.
Specifies the email address(es) of the licensed users to retrieve. This parameter filters users based on their email field.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the email address(es) of the licensed users to retrieve. This parameter filters users based on their email field.
Specifies the name(s) of the licensed users to retrieve. This parameter filters users based on their name field.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath drive types
- The orphan property indicates whether the user account still exists in the system (false = active, true = orphaned)
- userBundleLicenses contains license codes (e.g., "ATTUNU", "RPADEVNU")
- userBundleLicenseNames contains human-readable license names (e.g., "Attended - Named User")
- Use filtering to distinguish between active and orphaned user accounts
- lastInUse timestamp shows when the user last accessed UiPath services
- Platform Management cmdlets (starting with "Pm") provide cross-organizational license information

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves information about all licensed users in the Platform Management system.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 2: Get licensed user by email ------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves license information for multiple users by their email addresses.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 3: Get license details and examine structure -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first licensed user record and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 4: Get licensed users from specific drives ------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves licensed user information from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 5: Filter active licensed users -----------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-PmLicensedUser | Where-Object {$_.orphan -eq $false} | Select-Object displayName, email, userBundleLicenseNames, lastInUse

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all licensed users, filters for non-orphaned (active) users, and displays key information including license types.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 6: Group users by license type ------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-PmLicensedUser | Where-Object {$_.orphan -eq $false} | ForEach-Object { $_.userBundleLicenseNames } | Group-Object | Sort-Object Count -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets active licensed users and groups them by license type to show license distribution.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets robot accounts from Platform Management.

The Get-PmRobotAccount cmdlet retrieves robot account information from UiPath Platform Management. This cmdlet operates at the organization level and manages robot service accounts across the organization.

This is an organization entity cmdlet that calls the Platform Management API. It operates at the organization level, where multiple tenants can belong to the same organization. Robot accounts are service accounts used for automation processes and robot authentication across all tenants within the organization.

Platform Management robot accounts provide centralized identity management for automation scenarios, enabling secure and consistent robot authentication across the entire organization. These accounts are shared across tenants within the same organization to improve performance and reduce memory usage.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/robotaccounts
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]
Required permissions: Administration.View
Specifies the name of the target tenant drives. The robot account data is organization-wide regardless of which tenant drive is used.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets users from Platform Management.

The Get-PmUser cmdlet retrieves user information from the UiPath Platform Management service. This cmdlet accesses user data across the entire platform organization, including user accounts, email addresses, display names, group memberships, and account status.

Platform Management users are organization-level entities that can be assigned to multiple tenants and have access to various UiPath services. This cmdlet provides comprehensive user information including their group memberships, account status, and user types.
The cmdlet supports exporting user data to CSV format, which can be useful for user management, auditing, and integration with external systems.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/User/users/{partitionGlobalId}
OAuth required scopes: PM.User or PM.User.Read
Required permissions: Platform Management user read permissions

Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management commands work across all drive types (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:) as they access organization-level data.

Specifies the email addresses of the users to be retrieved. This parameter also accepts the alias "UserName". Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching to find users by email domain or partial email addresses.
Specifies the character encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. If not specified, UTF-8 with BOM is used by default, which ensures proper display in Excel and other applications.

Specifies the path where to export user data as a CSV file. The exported CSV contains user information in a format suitable for import operations and includes columns like Email, Name, SurName, DisplayName, Type, BypassBasicAuthRestriction, InvitationAccepted, and GroupName. The CSV uses UTF-8 encoding with BOM by default.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the character encoding for the exported CSV file when using -ExportCsv. If not specified, UTF-8 with BOM is used by default, which ensures proper display in Excel and other applications.

Specifies the email addresses of the users to be retrieved. This parameter also accepts the alias "UserName". Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching to find users by email domain or partial email addresses.

Specifies the path where to export user data as a CSV file. The exported CSV contains user information in a format suitable for import operations and includes columns like Email, Name, SurName, DisplayName, Type, BypassBasicAuthRestriction, InvitationAccepted, and GroupName. The CSV uses UTF-8 encoding with BOM by default.

Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management commands work across all drive types (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:) as they access organization-level data.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs, which work across all drive types (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:) since they operate at the organization level rather than tenant level.
The -ExportCsv parameter produces CSV files with UTF-8 BOM encoding by default, ensuring compatibility with Excel and other applications without character encoding issues.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of user objects, as they contain nested arrays like groupIDs that may not be visible in the default display format.
For user management operations, the exported CSV can be modified and used with corresponding import or update commands.
When working with large numbers of users, consider using Where-Object to filter results based on specific criteria like active status or group membership.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all users from the Platform Management service.
Retrieves the specific user with the email address "john.doe@company.com".
Retrieves all users whose email addresses belong to the "company.com" domain using wildcard matching.
Retrieves users from multiple Orchestrator environments (Orch1 and Orch2).

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-PmUser | Where-Object {$_.isActive -eq $true} | Select-Object userName, displayName, lastLoginTime

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves only active users and displays their username, display name, and last login time.
Retrieves all users and displays their complete structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.
Exports all Platform Management users to a CSV file that can be used for user management or import operations.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the Test Manager configuration settings.

The `Get-TmConfiguration` cmdlet retrieves the configuration settings for UiPath Test Manager. This includes endpoint configurations, basic settings, identity server configuration, storage configuration, search configuration, platform configuration, and various third-party integrations such as Azure DevOps, Jira, ServiceNow, and others.
The configuration object contains detailed information about how Test Manager is configured to integrate with external systems and manage test automation workflows.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - Test Manager configuration API endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Test Manager configuration scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Test Manager configuration permissions]
Specifies the path(s) to the Test Manager configuration. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards. If not specified, the configuration for the current location is retrieved.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the path(s) to the Test Manager configuration. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards. If not specified, the configuration for the current location is retrieved.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet requires access to a Test Manager instance
- The configuration object contains settings for various integrations including Azure DevOps, Jira (Server, Cloud Basic Auth, Cloud OAuth), ServiceNow, WebHook, XRay, Redmine, qTest, and SAP
- Some configuration properties may be null if the corresponding integrations are not configured

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1Tm: PS Orch1Tm:\> Get-TmConfiguration

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the complete Test Manager configuration for the current Test Manager instance.
Retrieves the Test Manager configuration and then displays only the basic configuration settings.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1Tm:\> Get-TmConfiguration | Select-Object endpoints, platformConfiguration

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the Test Manager configuration and displays only the endpoints and platform configuration properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets user permissions for projects in UiPath Test Manager.

The Get-TmProjectPermission cmdlet retrieves user permission information for Test Manager projects. This includes details about which users have access to specific projects, their assigned roles, ownership status, and permission modification history.

This cmdlet operates on the UiPathOrchTm provider and is a folder entity operation. You must either navigate to a specific Test Manager project folder using Set-Location (cd) or use the -Path or -Recurse parameters to specify target folders.

This cmdlet operates on the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider. If the scope in the configuration file includes "TM.", the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider will be automatically added. You can confirm this with the Get-PSDrive cmdlet. The configuration file can be opened with the Edit-OrchConfig cmdlet.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - GET /testmanager_/api/v2/{projectId}/permissions/project]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - TM.Projects or TM.Projects.Read]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - ProjectSettings.Update]

Specifies the target folder paths for Test Manager projects. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific project folders without changing the current location.
Searches for project permissions in all subfolders recursively. When specified, the cmdlet searches through the entire Test Manager project hierarchy.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

Specifies the target folder paths for Test Manager projects. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific project folders without changing the current location.
Searches for project permissions in all subfolders recursively. When specified, the cmdlet searches through the entire Test Manager project hierarchy.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

- Requires access to Test Manager functionality and appropriate TM scopes in configuration
- For optimal performance, place -Path and -Recurse parameters immediately after the cmdlet name to enable proper autocompletion
- Common roles include "Project Owner", "Test Manager", and "Viewer"
- The user object contains detailed information about the assigned user including email, display name, and identity information
- isOwner property indicates whether the user has ownership privileges for the project
- Use filtering operations to analyze permission patterns and identify security compliance issues

This cmdlet operates on the UiPathOrchTm provider and is a folder entity operation. You must either navigate to a specific Test Manager project folder using Set-Location (cd) or use the -Path or -Recurse parameters to specify target folders.

This cmdlet operates on the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider. If the scope in the configuration file includes "TM.", the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider will be automatically added. maml:description.maml:para: Gets the server information of Test Manager.

The `Get-TmServerInfo` cmdlet retrieves server information from UiPath Test Manager, including the version, server type, and operational status. This information is useful for verifying connectivity, checking server health, and confirming the Test Manager version.

This cmdlet operates on the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider. If the scope in the configuration file includes "TM.", the PSDrive of the UiPathOrchTm provider will be automatically added. You can confirm this with the Get-PSDrive cmdlet. The configuration file can be opened with the Edit-OrchConfig cmdlet.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /testmanager_/api/serverinfo
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Test Manager server info scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Test Manager server info permissions]
Specifies the name of the target Test Manager drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Test Manager instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target Test Manager drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Test Manager instances.

- The UiPathOrchTm PSDrive is automatically added when "TM." scope is included in the configuration
- Use Edit-OrchConfig to modify the configuration file if needed
- The status property indicates server health ("OK" for healthy servers)
- Server version information is useful for compatibility checks and troubleshooting

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test cases from projects in Test Manager.

The Get-TmTestCase cmdlet retrieves test cases from Test Manager projects. Test cases are individual test scenarios that can be either manual or automated, defining specific test steps, expected results, and automation associations.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires navigation to specific Test Manager projects or the use of -Path, -Recurse parameters. It works with the UiPathOrchTm provider, which is automatically available when Test Manager scopes are included in the configuration.

Test cases can be associated with automation projects and contain information about test execution parameters, preconditions, and version history. They serve as the foundation for test execution within test sets and provide detailed test documentation and traceability.
Primary Endpoint: GET /testmanager_/api/v2/{projectId}/testcases
OAuth required scopes: TM.TestCases or TM.TestCases.Read
Required permissions: TestCase.Read

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test cases. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific Test Manager projects without changing the current location.
Recursively gets test cases from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire Test Manager project hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies the names of the test cases to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test cases will be returned.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the names of the test cases to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test cases will be returned.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test cases. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific Test Manager projects without changing the current location.
Recursively gets test cases from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire Test Manager project hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific Test Manager projects or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
For Test Manager operations, ensure you are connected to the appropriate Test Manager drive (Orch1Tm:, Orch2Tm:, etc.) or use the -Path parameter to specify the correct drive.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large Test Manager environments, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Path to limit the scope if needed.
Test cases contain important information including: - automationTestCaseName: The associated automation workflow file
- automationProjectName: The automation project containing the test
- version: The test case version for change tracking
- preCondition: Prerequisites for test execution
- inputParams: Parameters required for test execution
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of test case objects, as they contain detailed metadata that may not be visible in the default display format.
Parameter placement is important for folder entity cmdlets. Place -Path, -Recurse parameters immediately after the cmdlet name for proper auto-completion functionality.

Test cases can be either manual (no automation association) or automated (linked to UiPath workflows). Use filtering to distinguish between these types based on the presence of automationTestCaseName.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all test cases from all Test Manager projects recursively.
Gets test cases whose names contain "login" using wildcard pattern matching.
Gets test cases from multiple Test Manager instances.
Gets all test cases and displays the complete structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test sets from projects in Test Manager.

The Get-TmTestSet cmdlet retrieves test sets from Test Manager projects. Test sets are collections of test cases that can be executed together as a group, providing organized test execution and reporting capabilities.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires navigation to specific Test Manager projects or the use of -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. It works with the UiPathOrchTm provider, which is automatically available when Test Manager scopes are included in the configuration.

Test sets can originate from various sources including Orchestrator processes, external test management systems, or manual test creation. The cmdlet provides information about test set configuration, execution order enforcement, coverage settings, and associated test cases.
Primary Endpoint: GET /testmanager_/api/v2/{projectId}/testsets
OAuth required scopes: TM.TestSets or TM.TestSets.Read
Required permissions: TestSet.Read

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test sets. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific Test Manager projects without changing the current location.
Recursively gets test sets from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire Test Manager project hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies the names of the test sets to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test sets will be returned.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

For Test Manager operations, ensure you are connected to the appropriate Test Manager drive (Orch1Tm:, Orch2Tm:, etc.) or use the -Path parameter to specify the correct drive.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large Test Manager environments, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Path to limit the scope if needed.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports library packages into UiPath Orchestrator.

The Import-OrchLibrary cmdlet imports library packages (.nupkg files) into UiPath Orchestrator. Libraries are reusable components that contain activities, workflows, and other resources that can be shared across different processes. This cmdlet allows you to upload and deploy library packages to make them available for use in automation projects.
The cmdlet returns import status information for each library, including success or failure details.
The -Path parameter specifies target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
This is a tenant-level operation that imports libraries to the Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.UploadPackage
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution
Required permissions: Libraries.Create
Specifies the file paths of library packages (.nupkg files) to import. Multiple file paths can be specified as an array. The files must exist and be valid UiPath library packages.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows importing libraries to multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This provides an additional safety check before importing libraries.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the import operation. This is useful for verifying which libraries would be imported before making changes.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows importing libraries to multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the file paths of library packages (.nupkg files) to import. Multiple file paths can be specified as an array. The files must exist and be valid UiPath library packages.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This provides an additional safety check before importing libraries.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the import operation. This is useful for verifying which libraries would be imported before making changes.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Library files must be valid .nupkg packages created by UiPath Studio or other UiPath tools
- The Status property in the result indicates whether the import was successful ("OK") or failed
- Importing a library that already exists with the same version may result in different behavior depending on Orchestrator configuration
- Use -WhatIf parameter to preview import operations before executing
- The Body property contains JSON-formatted details about the imported library including Id and Version
- Large library files may take longer to import depending on network speed and package size
- This cmdlet operates at the tenant level and imports libraries to the entire Orchestrator instance

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports all library *.nupkg files in the current location of C: drive into the current Orchestrator tenant.
Imports all library *.nupkg files in the specified folder into the current Orchestrator tenant.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports packages from local files to specified folders.

The Import-OrchPackage cmdlet imports automation packages from local .nupkg files into UiPath Orchestrator folders. This cmdlet enables deployment of automation packages from backup files, migration between environments, or installation of packages created offline.

Packages contain compiled automation workflows and their dependencies. Importing packages uploads .nupkg files to Orchestrator, making them available for process deployment and execution. This is essential for environment setup, disaster recovery, and automation deployment scenarios.

Use the -Source parameter to specify the local .nupkg files or directories containing packages to import. The -Path parameter specifies the target Orchestrator folders where packages should be uploaded. The cmdlet supports wildcard patterns for importing multiple packages efficiently.

This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. The -Recurse parameter enables processing all subdirectories when importing from local directories.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Processes/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.UploadPackage
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution
Required permissions: Packages.Create and FolderPackages.Create
Specifies the source .nupkg files or directories containing packages to be imported.
Specifies the target folders where packages should be imported. If not specified, the current folder will be used as the target.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies that all subdirectories should be processed when importing from local directories.
Specifies the target folders where packages should be imported. If not specified, the current folder will be used as the target.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the source .nupkg files or directories containing packages to be imported.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies that all subdirectories should be processed when importing from local directories.

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a folder entity cmdlet. Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

Imported packages must be valid .nupkg files created by UiPath Studio or exported using Export-OrchPackage. Ensure package dependencies are available in the target environment. Package names and versions must be unique within folders. Use wildcards for efficient bulk operations and -WhatIf for testing before actual execution.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports a package from the local file system to the Orchestrator tenant feed.
Imports a package from the local file system to the Orchestrator folder feed. If there is no feed in the Orch1:\Development folder, it will be imported to the tenant feed.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\LocalFeed> Import-OrchPackage MyProcess.1.0.1.nupkg -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports the same package to the tenant feeds of multiple Orchestrator instances.

Bulk imports all package files in the folder. The import destination will be the current folder of the Orch1: drive. If you want to always import to the tenant feed regardless of the current folder location, specify Orch1:\ in the -Path parameter.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports queue items from CSV files to specified queues.

The Import-OrchQueueItem cmdlet imports queue items from CSV files into UiPath Orchestrator queues within specified folders. This cmdlet enables bulk loading of work items from external data sources, making it essential for migrating data, setting up test environments, or loading work items from external systems.

Queue items represent units of work that robots process sequentially. Importing queue items from CSV files allows for efficient bulk data loading, migration from other systems, or preparation of test data for automation scenarios.

Use the -Name parameter to specify which queues should receive the imported items. The -ImportCsv parameter specifies the CSV files containing the queue item data. The cmdlet supports various CSV encodings and commit strategies for different import scenarios.

This cmdlet operates on folders. Use -Path parameter to specify target folders where the queues are located. The CSV files must contain properly formatted queue item data with appropriate column headers.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueDefinitions, POST /odata/Queues/UiPathODataSvc.BulkAddQueueItems
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Write
Required permissions: Queues.Edit
Specifies the target folders containing the queues. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the names of the queues where items should be imported.
Specifies the encoding of the CSV files. If not specified, UTF8 encoding will be used.
Specifies the commit strategy for the import operation. Valid values include Immediate, Batch, or Transaction.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the CSV files containing queue item data to be imported.
Specifies the commit strategy for the import operation. Valid values include Immediate, Batch, or Transaction.
Specifies the encoding of the CSV files. If not specified, UTF8 encoding will be used.
Specifies the names of the queues where items should be imported.
Specifies the target folders containing the queues. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the CSV files containing queue item data to be imported.

maml:alert.maml:para: CSV files must contain properly formatted queue item data with appropriate column headers. The columns should match the queue item properties and specific data expected by the target queue. Ensure CSV files are properly encoded to avoid data corruption during import.

Import operations can be time-consuming for large CSV files. Use appropriate commit strategies based on data volume and transaction requirements. Test imports with -WhatIf before executing large data imports.

maml:description.maml:para: Moves folder users between folders.
The Move-OrchFolderUser cmdlet moves users from one folder to another, transferring their access rights and role assignments between folder contexts.
This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.

By default, moving a user removes their access from the source folder and grants access to the destination folder. Use -KeepSource to maintain access to the original folder while adding access to the destination folder.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/FolderUsers/Move
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Write
Required permissions: Folders.Edit
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Specifies the names of the users to be moved between folders.
Specifies the destination folder path where users will be moved.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies whether to keep the user in the source folder after moving to the destination. Set to "true" to maintain dual folder access.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Moves group members between Platform Management groups.
The Move-PmGroupMember cmdlet moves members from one Platform Management group to another. This cmdlet operates at the organization level and manages group memberships across the organization.

This is an organization entity cmdlet that calls the Platform Management API. It operates at the organization level, where multiple tenants can belong to the same organization. Group memberships are managed centrally and affect access across all tenants within the organization.

By default, moving a member removes them from the source group and adds them to the destination group. Use -KeepSource to maintain membership in the original group while adding to the destination group.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/Directory/BulkResolveByName/{tenantId}, GET /api/Group/{tenantId}/{groupId}, PUT /api/Group/{groupId}

maml:description.maml:para: Creates new storage buckets.

The New-OrchBucket cmdlet creates new storage buckets in UiPath Orchestrator. Buckets provide file storage capabilities for automation processes, allowing them to store, retrieve, and manage files during execution. This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using the -Path parameter. If you attempt to run this cmdlet without being in a folder context, you will receive the error: "Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters."

Buckets can be configured with various storage providers including FileSystem, Azure Blob Storage, Amazon S3, and others. You can specify custom storage containers, credential stores for secure authentication, and organize buckets using tags. Options allow you to control bucket behavior such as read-only access or encryption settings.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Buckets OAuth required scopes: OR.Buckets or OR.Buckets.Write Required permissions: Buckets.Create
Specifies the path(s) to the target folder(s) where the bucket(s) will be created. Use this parameter to create buckets in specific folders without changing your current location.

Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the credential store to use for authentication with the storage provider.
Specifies a description for the bucket that explains its purpose or contents.
Specifies an external name for the bucket, useful when the bucket needs to be referenced differently in external systems.
Specifies bucket options such as ReadOnly, Encrypted, or other provider-specific options.
Specifies the password for authentication with the storage provider.
Specifies the storage container or path where the bucket data will be stored. The format depends on the storage provider.
Specifies additional storage-specific parameters as a JSON string or parameter string.
Specifies the storage provider to use for the bucket. Common providers include FileSystem, Azure, AmazonS3, and others.
Specifies tags to associate with the bucket for organization, categorization, and management purposes.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates machines.

The New-OrchMachine cmdlet creates new machines in UiPath Orchestrator. Machines define the execution environments where robots can run automation processes. You can configure various types of machines including Standard machines for production workloads and Template machines for creating standardized configurations. This is a tenant entity cmdlet. You can specify target tenants using the -Path parameter with drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:). If -Path is not specified, the machine will be created in the current location.

Machines can be configured with different slot types for various automation scenarios: UnattendedSlots for background automation, NonProductionSlots for development and testing, and TestAutomationSlots for automated testing scenarios. You can also specify automation types (Any, Foreground, Background) and target frameworks (Portable, Windows).
The cmdlet supports CSV import functionality. Use Get-OrchMachine -ExportCsv to obtain the format for bulk machine creation.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Machines OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Write Required permissions: Machines.Create
Specifies the path(s) to the target tenant(s) where the machine(s) will be created. Use drive names like Orch1:, Orch2: to target specific tenants.
Specifies the name(s) of the machine(s) to create. Machine names must be unique within the tenant.

Specifies the automation type supported by the machine. Valid values are "Any" (supports both foreground and background), "Foreground" (requires user interaction), or "Background" (runs without user interaction).
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies a description for the machine that explains its purpose or configuration.
Specifies the number of non-production slots for development and testing purposes. These slots are typically used in development environments.
Specifies the robot users who are authorized to execute automation on this machine. Use domain\username format for domain users.
Specifies the scope of the machine. This determines the visibility and access level of the machine within the organization.
Specifies tags to associate with the machine for organization, categorization, and management purposes.
Specifies the target framework for the machine. Valid values include "Portable" (cross-platform) and "Windows" (Windows-specific framework).
Specifies the number of test automation slots for automated testing scenarios. These slots are dedicated to running test cases and test sets.
Specifies the type of machine to create. Valid values are "Standard" for regular machines and "Template" for template machines used to standardize configurations.
Specifies the number of unattended slots for background automation. These slots allow robots to run without user interaction.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

- Consider using -WhatIf to preview the operation before actual creation
- Slot allocation affects licensing and should be planned according to your automation needs
- Template machines can be used to standardize machine configurations across environments
- Robot users must exist in the system before being assigned to machines
- Tags are useful for organizing machines and implementing governance policies

maml:description.maml:para: Creates automation processes from published packages.

The New-OrchProcess cmdlet creates automation processes from published packages in UiPath Orchestrator. A process is a deployable automation workflow that can be executed by robots. This cmdlet allows you to configure process settings including entry points, input arguments, priority levels, recording options, and retention policies. This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. If you attempt to run this cmdlet without being in a folder context, you will receive the error: \"Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.\"

The process is created from an existing package identified by its Id. You can specify a particular version or use the latest version by default. Process configuration includes execution settings, monitoring options, and lifecycle management policies such as retention rules for job artifacts.
The cmdlet supports CSV import functionality. Use Get-OrchProcess -Recurse -ExportCsv to obtain the format for bulk process creation.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Releases/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.CreateRelease OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Write Required permissions: Execution.Create

maml:description.maml:para: Creates queues.

Creates new queues in UiPath Orchestrator folders. Queues are data repositories that enable robots to exchange information and work items in an organized manner, supporting both attended and unattended automation scenarios.
This cmdlet supports CSV import functionality. The format of the importable CSV can be obtained using Get-OrchQueue -Recurse -ExportCsv. This allows for bulk queue creation and configuration.
Queue entities are folder-scoped. You must navigate to a folder or use -Path parameters to specify target folders.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/QueueDefinitions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.CreateQueue, GET /odata/Releases, GET /odata/Buckets
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues.Write
Required permissions: Queues.Create
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates automation triggers for process execution.

The New-OrchTrigger cmdlet creates automation triggers that automatically start process executions based on various conditions. Triggers can be time-based (scheduled), queue-based (activated by queue items), or event-based (triggered by specific conditions). This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using the -Path parameter. If you attempt to run this cmdlet without being in a folder context, you will receive the error: \"Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.\"

Triggers support various execution strategies including time-based scheduling using Cron expressions, queue-based activation when items are added to queues, and advanced configurations such as calendar integration, robot assignment, and failure handling policies.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/ProcessSchedules OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Write Required permissions: Execution.Create
Specifies the path(s) to the target folder(s) where the trigger(s) will be created. Use this parameter to create triggers in specific folders without changing your current location.
Specifies the name(s) of the trigger(s) to create. Trigger names must be unique within the folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates groups in Platform Management.

The New-PmGroup cmdlet creates groups in UiPath Platform Management. Groups are used to organize users and manage permissions at the organization level across multiple tenants. This is an organization entity cmdlet. It operates at the Platform Management level and manages groups across the entire organization. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants if needed.

Groups created through Platform Management provide centralized user and permission management capabilities that span multiple tenants within the same organization, as mentioned in the UiPathOrch 0.9.13.0 release notes regarding shared caches across tenants.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/Group OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Write Required permissions: Users.Create
Specifies the path(s) to target tenant(s). Use drive names like Orch1:, Orch2: to specify tenant context.
Specifies the name(s) of the group(s) to create. Group names must be unique within the organization.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates users in Platform Management.

The New-PmUser cmdlet creates users in UiPath Platform Management. Users created at the Platform Management level have organization-wide access and can be managed centrally across multiple tenants. This is an organization entity cmdlet. It operates at the Platform Management level and manages users across the entire organization. Use the -Path parameter to specify target tenants if needed.

Users created through Platform Management benefit from shared caches across tenants within the same organization, as mentioned in the UiPathOrch 0.9.13.0 release notes, providing improved performance and consistent behavior.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/platformmanagement/users OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Write Required permissions: Users.Create
Specifies the path(s) to target tenant(s). Use drive names like Orch1:, Orch2: to specify tenant context.

maml:description.maml:para: Opens job details in the default web browser.

The `Open-OrchJob` cmdlet opens the job details page in the default web browser for the specified job ID(s). This provides a convenient way to view detailed job information, execution logs, and other job-related data directly in the Orchestrator web interface.
This cmdlet is useful for quickly navigating from PowerShell to the web interface to investigate job details, view execution logs, or access features not available through the command line.
Multiple job IDs can be specified to open multiple job pages simultaneously. You can use autocomplete for job IDs by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: Browser navigation to job details page (no direct API endpoint)
OAuth required scopes: Not applicable (browser navigation)
Required permissions: Jobs.View (for browser access to job details)
Specifies the target folder path(s). If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies the job ID(s) to open in the browser. You can specify multiple job IDs to open multiple pages. The completion list for this parameter includes job IDs that have been previously retrieved and cached in memory by UiPathOrch.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

Specifies the job ID(s) to open in the browser. You can specify multiple job IDs to open multiple pages. The completion list for this parameter includes job IDs that have been previously retrieved and cached in memory by UiPathOrch.
Specifies the target folder path(s). If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This is a utility cmdlet for navigating from PowerShell to the Orchestrator web interface
- The cmdlet requires an active web browser and internet connectivity to the Orchestrator instance
- Multiple job IDs will open multiple browser tabs or windows depending on browser settings
- The user must have appropriate permissions to view job details in the web interface

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\MyWorkspace PS Orch1:\MyWorkspace> Open-OrchJob -Id 108984122

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the job details page for job ID 108984122 in the default web browser.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\MyWorkspace> $job = Get-OrchJob -First 1 PS Orch1:\MyWorkspace> Open-OrchJob -Id $job.Id

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the first job and opens its details page in the browser.
Opens job details pages for multiple jobs in separate browser tabs or windows.
Gets the first 5 faulted jobs and opens their details pages for investigation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\MyWorkspace> Get-OrchJob -Last "1h" | Where-Object State -eq "Faulted" | Open-OrchJob

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens job details pages for all jobs that failed in the last hour.

maml:description.maml:para: Opens the folder where the HTTP logs of UiPathOrch cmdlets are output.

The `Open-OrchLogLocation` cmdlet opens the folder containing HTTP request/response logs generated by UiPathOrch module cmdlets. These logs are useful for debugging and troubleshooting API communication with UiPath Orchestrator.
By default, the cmdlet opens the system's default log location. You can optionally specify a custom path using the `-Path` parameter to open a different location.
The log files contain detailed information about HTTP requests and responses exchanged between the UiPathOrch PowerShell module and the UiPath Orchestrator web API endpoints.
Primary Endpoint: Local utility function (no API endpoint)
OAuth required scopes: Not applicable (local utility function)
Required permissions: Not applicable (local utility function)
Specifies an alternative folder path to open instead of the default log location. If not specified, the cmdlet opens the default system log folder for UiPathOrch HTTP logs.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies an alternative folder path to open instead of the default log location. If not specified, the cmdlet opens the default system log folder for UiPathOrch HTTP logs.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This is a utility cmdlet for accessing log files generated by other UiPathOrch cmdlets
- The actual log files contain HTTP request/response details for debugging purposes
- Log files may contain sensitive information such as authentication tokens

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the default log folder location where UiPathOrch HTTP logs are stored.
Opens the specified custom folder path instead of the default log location.

maml:description.maml:para: Retries failed transaction items in the specified queues.

This cmdlet retries transaction items by specifying the queue name and the item IDs. It retries only retryable items, defined as those with a Status of Failed and a Revision of either None or InReview.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/QueueItems/UiPathODataSvc.SetItemReviewStatus
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues
Required permissions: Queues.View and Transactions.Edit

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retries the specified items in the queue. The -Id parameter, which specifies <item IDs>, supports auto-completion.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchQueueItem YourQueueName -Status Failed -Revision None,InReview | Redo-OrchQueueItem -Verbose

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retries all failed items in the specified queue, YourQueueName. The -Verbose parameter displays the IDs of the retried items.

Please note that the Get-OrchQueueItem cmdlet can retrieve up to a maximum of 1000 items at a time. If there are more than 1,000 retriable items in the queue, repeat this command until the -Verbose parameter stops producing output to retry all of these items.
Retries all retryable queue items across all queues in the tenant.
