maml:description.maml:para: Removes assets from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Remove-OrchAsset` cmdlet removes one or more assets from UiPath Orchestrator. Assets are data stores that contain configuration settings, credentials, and other data used by automation processes.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either navigation to the target folder or specification of target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. Use caution when removing assets as this action cannot be undone and may affect automation processes that depend on these assets.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Assets({id})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Assets or OR.Assets.Write
Required permissions: Assets.Delete
Specifies the folder path(s) from which to remove assets. Supports wildcards and multiple values. If not specified, the current location is used.
Includes assets from subfolders when removing assets. This is a folder entity operation parameter.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the name(s) of the assets to remove. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the value type(s) of assets to remove. Valid values include: Text, Bool, Integer, Credential. Use this parameter to limit removal to specific asset types.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by the cmdlet.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the name(s) of the assets to remove. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the folder path(s) from which to remove assets. Supports wildcards and multiple values. If not specified, the current location is used.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by the cmdlet.
Includes assets from subfolders when removing assets. This is a folder entity operation parameter.
Specifies the value type(s) of assets to remove. Valid values include: Text, Bool, Integer, Credential. Use this parameter to limit removal to specific asset types.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either: - Navigation to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), OR

- Specification of target folders using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth before other parameters when using multiple parameters. Security and Safety Considerations: - Asset removal is permanent and cannot be undone
- Removing assets may break automation processes that depend on them
- Always use -WhatIf to preview deletions before execution
- Use -Confirm for interactive confirmation of each deletion
- Consider backing up critical assets before removal Asset Types That Can Be Removed: - Text: String values for configuration settings
- Bool: Boolean values for feature flags
- Integer: Numeric values for thresholds and counts
- Credential: Encrypted username/password pairs Best Practices: - Test removal operations in development environments first
- Use specific asset names rather than broad wildcards
- Document removed assets for audit purposes
- Coordinate with process owners before removing shared assets
- Consider using Get-OrchAsset with Where-Object to preview targets Common Use Cases: - Cleanup of test assets after development cycles
- Removal of deprecated configuration values
- Cleanup of assets with specific naming patterns
- Batch removal based on modification dates
Use Get-OrchAsset to view available assets before removal and verify the scope of the deletion operation.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows what would happen when removing the "TestAsset" without actually performing the deletion.
Removes the "TempAsset" with a confirmation prompt before deletion.
Removes all assets whose names start with "Config" from the Development folder without changing the current location.
Removes all Text-type assets whose names start with "Test" from all folders recursively.
Shows what would happen when removing assets that haven't been modified in the last 30 days.
Removes all assets whose names start with "Deprecated" from all folders with confirmation for each deletion.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes storage buckets.

The Remove-OrchBucket cmdlet removes storage buckets from UiPath Orchestrator. This operation permanently deletes the bucket and any contained folders or files, so use with caution. This is a folder entity cmdlet. To use this cmdlet, you must first navigate to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), or specify the target folders using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters. If you attempt to run this cmdlet without being in a folder context, you will receive the error: \"Use Set-Location cmdlet (cd command) to navigate to the target folder first, or specify the target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.\" Warning : This operation is destructive and cannot be undone. All data stored in the bucket will be permanently deleted. Consider using -WhatIf to preview the operation before execution, and -Confirm for additional safety.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Buckets({id}) OAuth required scopes: OR.Administration or OR.Administration.Write Required permissions: Buckets.Delete
Specifies the path(s) to the target folder(s) containing the buckets to remove. Use this parameter to remove buckets from specific folders without changing your current location.
Indicates that the cmdlet should process all subfolders recursively when used with -Path.
Specifies the maximum depth of recursion when used with -Recurse parameter.
Specifies the name(s) of the bucket(s) to remove. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. Recommended for production environments.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. This is highly recommended for destructive operations.

Specifies the name(s) of the bucket(s) to remove. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Specifies the path(s) to the target folder(s) containing the buckets to remove. Use this parameter to remove buckets from specific folders without changing your current location.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: - DESTRUCTIVE OPERATION : This command permanently deletes buckets and all their contents - Always use -WhatIf first to preview the operation
- Consider using -Confirm for additional safety in production environments
- Wildcard patterns allow bulk deletion but require extra caution
- Pipeline processing enables complex filtering scenarios
- Ensure proper backup procedures before removing important buckets

maml:description.maml:para: Removes calendars from the Orchestrator.

The Remove-OrchCalendar cmdlet permanently removes calendars from the Orchestrator environment. Calendars define business schedules and time zones for automation processes and can include business days, holidays, and specific time periods.

When a calendar is removed, all its associated schedule definitions, business days, holidays, and time zone settings are permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone, so careful consideration should be taken before removing calendars, especially those that are actively used by triggers or scheduled processes.

The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before deletion. It is strongly recommended to use these parameters when removing calendars to avoid accidental deletion of critical scheduling configurations.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/Calendars({calendarId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings
Required permissions: Settings.Delete
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove calendars from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies the names of the calendars to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching calendars will be removed.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing calendars as the operation cannot be undone and affects scheduling configurations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which calendars would be removed before actually performing the operation.
Specifies the names of the calendars to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching calendars will be removed.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove calendars from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing calendars as the operation cannot be undone and affects scheduling configurations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which calendars would be removed before actually performing the operation.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects calendars across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.

Before removing calendars, ensure they are not being used by active triggers, schedules, or automation processes. You can use Get-OrchTrigger to check for triggers that may be using specific calendars.

Removing a calendar also removes all associated calendar dates, business days, and holiday definitions. If you need to preserve this information, consider copying the calendar configuration before removal.
Use the -WhatIf parameter to preview the operation before actual execution, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple calendars.
The removal operation is irreversible. Once a calendar is deleted, it cannot be recovered and must be recreated with all its schedule definitions if needed.
Calendars that are actively referenced by triggers or scheduled processes should not be removed as this may cause scheduling failures.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes credential stores from the Orchestrator.

The Remove-OrchCredentialStore cmdlet permanently removes credential stores from the Orchestrator environment. Credential stores are external systems that securely manage and provide credentials for automation processes, such as AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, or the built-in Orchestrator database.

When a credential store is removed, the connection to the external credential management system is permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone, so careful consideration should be taken before removing credential stores, especially those that are actively used by assets or automation processes.
Before removing a credential store, ensure that no assets are currently configured to use that store, as this will break credential access for those assets.
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /odata/CredentialStores({credentialStoreId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Write
Required permissions: Settings.Delete
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove credential stores from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.

Specifies the names of the credential stores to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching credential stores will be removed.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing credential stores as the operation cannot be undone and affects credential management.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which credential stores would be removed before actually performing the operation.

Specifies the names of the credential stores to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching credential stores will be removed.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove credential stores from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing credential stores as the operation cannot be undone and affects credential management.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which credential stores would be removed before actually performing the operation.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects credential stores across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
Before removing credential stores, ensure they are not being used by any assets. You can use Get-OrchAsset to check for assets that may be using specific credential stores.
The built-in "Orchestrator Database" credential store typically should not be removed as it may be required for basic credential functionality.

Removing external credential stores (AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, etc.) will break the connection to those external systems, but does not affect the credentials stored in the external systems themselves.
Use the -WhatIf parameter to preview the operation before actual execution, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple credential stores.
The removal operation is irreversible. Once a credential store is deleted, it cannot be recovered and must be recreated with all its configuration if needed.
Read-only credential stores may have special restrictions on removal depending on their configuration and usage.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the credential store named "TestStore" from the current Orchestrator environment.
Shows what would happen if the specified credential stores were removed, without actually performing the removal.
Removes all credential stores whose names start with "Test", prompting for confirmation before each removal.

command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Remove-OrchCredentialStore LegacyStore -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the credential store named "LegacyStore" from multiple Orchestrator environments.

Removes a credential store with confirmation prompt and suppresses the output.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes machines from the Orchestrator.

The Remove-OrchMachine cmdlet permanently removes machines from the Orchestrator environment. Machines represent physical or virtual computers that can host UiPath robots and execute automation processes.

When a machine is removed, all robots associated with that machine are also removed from the Orchestrator. This action cannot be undone, so careful consideration should be taken before removing machines, especially those that are actively used for automation execution.

The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before deletion. It is strongly recommended to use these parameters when removing machines to avoid accidental deletion of critical automation infrastructure.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Machines, DELETE /odata/Machines({machineId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Write
Required permissions: Machines.Delete
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove machines from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies the names of the machines to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching machines will be removed.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing machines as the operation cannot be undone and affects all robots on those machines.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which machines would be removed before actually performing the operation.
Specifies the names of the machines to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching machines will be removed.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove machines from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing machines as the operation cannot be undone and affects all robots on those machines.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which machines would be removed before actually performing the operation.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects machines across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
Before removing machines, ensure they are not actively running processes or jobs. You can use Get-OrchJob to check for running jobs on specific machines.

Removing a machine also removes all robots associated with that machine. If you need to preserve robot configurations, consider using Copy-OrchMachine to backup the machine configuration before removal.
Template machines and system-generated machines should be removed with extreme caution as they may be required for proper functioning of the Orchestrator environment.
Use the -WhatIf parameter to preview the operation before actual execution, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple machines.
The removal operation is irreversible. Once a machine is deleted, it cannot be recovered and must be recreated if needed.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes personal workspace folders from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Remove-OrchPersonalWorkspace cmdlet permanently removes personal workspace folders from UiPath Orchestrator. Personal workspaces are dedicated folders that provide users with individual environments for developing, testing, and managing their automation projects.

This cmdlet removes the actual folder structure and all contained content, including processes, assets, and other automation artifacts stored within the personal workspace. This operation is irreversible and permanently deletes the workspace data.

The cmdlet operates at the folder level within Orchestrator, targeting specific personal workspace folders by name or owner. Use the -Name parameter to specify workspace folder names directly, or the -OwnerName parameter to target workspaces by their owning user. Both parameters support wildcard patterns for bulk operations.

This is a folder-level entity operation that requires navigation to the appropriate folder path or specification of the target path using the -Path parameter. The operation requires appropriate permissions to delete folders within the Orchestrator environment.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/PersonalWorkspaces, DELETE /odata/Folders({folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders
Required permissions: Units.View, (Units.Delete or SubFolders.Delete)
Specifies the target tenant drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. For folder-level operations requiring path specification.
Specifies the name of the personal workspace folders to be removed. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Specifies the owner name (username) of the personal workspace folders to be removed. Supports wildcard patterns for bulk operations.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Always use -WhatIf to preview the operation before execution, especially when using wildcard patterns. The operation requires Units.Delete or SubFolders.Delete permissions depending on the folder hierarchy.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes queue items.

The Remove-OrchQueueItem cmdlet removes specific queue items by their IDs from queues within UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet is designed to work both independently and as part of queue item migration workflows.

Specify the queue name and the IDs of the items you want to delete. If you also provide the RowVersion, the deletion process will be faster. When RowVersion is not specified, it will be retrieved from the cache for the given ID. If the cache does not contain the RowVersion, the cmdlet will automatically call the GetItemById API to obtain it.

The cmdlet outputs any items that failed to be removed. These failed items can be exported to a CSV file using Export-Csv and later re-imported with Import-Csv to retry deletion. This provides resilient error handling for large-scale queue item management operations.

You can easily remove items that were successfully copied by piping the output of Copy-OrchQueueItem into Remove-OrchQueueItem. This creates a complete move operation that prevents duplicate transaction processing.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueItems({queueItemId}), POST /odata/QueueItems/UiPathODataSvc.DeleteBulk
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues
Required permissions: Queues.View and Transactions.Delete
Specifies the source folder containing the queue. If not specified, the current folder will be used.
Specifies the name of the queue containing the items to be removed.
Specifies the IDs of the queue items to be removed.
Specifies the RowVersion of the queue items for faster deletion. If not provided, it will be retrieved automatically.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the IDs of the queue items to be removed.
Specifies the name of the queue containing the items to be removed.
Specifies the source folder containing the queue. If not specified, the current folder will be used.
Specifies the RowVersion of the queue items for faster deletion. If not provided, it will be retrieved automatically.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet can be used independently by specifying queue name and item IDs, or as part of a pipeline with Copy-OrchQueueItem for move operations. Performance : Providing RowVersion accelerates deletion by avoiding additional API calls to retrieve version information. Error Handling : Failed removals are returned as output and can be exported to CSV for retry later. Use Import-Csv to reload failed items and pipe them back to Remove-OrchQueueItem. Move Operation Pattern : 1. `Copy-OrchQueueItem SourceQueue Destination | Remove-OrchQueueItem | Export-Csv failedRemovals.csv` 2. `Import-Csv failedRemovals.csv | Remove-OrchQueueItem` (retry failures)
Use -WhatIf to preview deletions before executing, especially for bulk operations.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes queue items with IDs 12345, 12346, and 12347 from MyQueue in the current folder.
Copies queue items from MyQueue to destination and removes the successfully copied items from the source queue, completing a move operation.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes roles from the Orchestrator.
Permanently removes roles from UiPath Orchestrator tenants. Roles define permissions and access levels for users and groups within the organization.
Only custom roles that are not currently assigned to users or groups can be removed. Static (built-in) roles cannot be deleted as they are essential for Orchestrator functionality.
The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview operations and -Confirm to request confirmation before deletion.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Roles, DELETE /odata/Roles({roleId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Roles.Delete
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Prompts for confirmation before running the cmdlet. Recommended for destructive operations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually performing the operation. Recommended for safety verification.
Specifies the names of roles to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies target tenants by drive name. Use comma-separated values for multiple tenants. If not specified, targets the current tenant.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Prompts for confirmation before running the cmdlet. Recommended for destructive operations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually performing the operation. Recommended for safety verification.
Specifies the names of roles to remove. Supports wildcards and multiple values.

maml:alert.maml:para: Role entities are tenant-scoped and operate across the entire tenant.
Before removing roles, ensure they are not assigned to any users or groups. Use Get-OrchRole to check assignments and Remove-OrchRoleFromUser or Remove-OrchRoleFromFolderUser to remove assignments.
Static roles (IsStatic = $true) cannot be removed as they are essential for Orchestrator functionality.
The removal operation is irreversible. Use -WhatIf to preview operations before execution.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows what would happen when removing the role without actually performing the deletion.
Removes roles whose names start with "Test", prompting for confirmation before each removal.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Remove-OrchRole TemporaryRole -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the role from multiple tenants.
Shows which custom (non-static) roles would be removed using pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes webhooks from the Orchestrator.
The Remove-OrchWebhook cmdlet permanently removes webhooks from the Orchestrator environment. Webhooks provide HTTP notifications to external systems when specific events occur in Orchestrator.

When a webhook is removed, all its configuration including URL, event types, and custom headers are permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone, so careful consideration should be taken before removing webhooks, especially those that are actively used for critical integrations.

The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before deletion. It is strongly recommended to use these parameters when removing webhooks to avoid accidental deletion of important integration configurations.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Webhooks, DELETE /odata/Webhooks({webhookId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks
Required permissions: Webhooks.Delete
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove webhooks from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be removed.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing webhooks as the operation cannot be undone and affects integration configurations.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be removed before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when removing webhooks as the operation cannot be undone and affects integration configurations.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be removed. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be removed.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to remove webhooks from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be removed before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects webhooks across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.

Before removing webhooks, consider disabling them first using Disable-OrchWebhook to test the impact on your integrations. This allows you to verify that the removal won't break critical business processes.
If you need to preserve webhook configurations for future use, consider documenting the webhook settings or exporting the configuration before removal.
Use the -WhatIf parameter to preview the operation before actual execution, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple webhooks.
The removal operation is irreversible. Once a webhook is deleted, it cannot be recovered and must be recreated with all its configuration if needed.

Removing webhooks may impact external systems that depend on Orchestrator notifications. Ensure that dependent systems are updated or have alternative notification mechanisms before removing webhooks.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the webhook named "TestWebhook" from the current Orchestrator environment.
Shows what would happen if the specified webhooks were removed, without actually performing the removal.
Removes all webhooks whose names start with "Temp", prompting for confirmation before each removal.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Remove-OrchWebhook OldIntegration -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the webhook named "OldIntegration" from multiple Orchestrator environments.
Identifies disabled webhooks containing "test" in their name and shows what would be removed without actually performing the deletion.
Removes a webhook with confirmation prompt and suppresses the output.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes groups from UiPath Platform Management.

The Remove-PmGroup cmdlet removes groups from UiPath Platform Management. Groups are collections of users that can be assigned roles and permissions collectively. This cmdlet permanently removes the specified groups and their associated configurations.
The -Path parameter specifies target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath Orchestrator drives (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /api/Group/{tenantId}/{groupId}
OAuth required scopes: PM.Group
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Platform Management group delete permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies the names of groups to remove. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Multiple group names can be specified as an array.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This provides an additional safety check before removing groups.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the removal operation. This is useful for verifying which groups would be affected before making changes.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This provides an additional safety check before removing groups.
Specifies the names of groups to remove. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Multiple group names can be specified as an array.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the removal operation. This is useful for verifying which groups would be affected before making changes.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet permanently removes groups and cannot be undone
- Use -WhatIf parameter to preview the removal operation before executing
- Removing a group will affect any users, roles, and permissions associated with it
- The -GroupName parameter supports wildcard characters for bulk operations
- Platform Management cmdlets (starting with "Pm") provide cross-organizational group management
- Always verify group dependencies and member assignments before removal
- Built-in system groups (like "Everyone") may be protected from deletion

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 1: Remove multiple groups --------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Remove-PmGroup TestGroup1, TestGroup2, CustomGroup

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes multiple groups in a single operation.
Shows what would happen if all groups starting with "Test" were removed.
Removes the group with an additional confirmation prompt before proceeding.

command:example.maml:title: -------- Example 4: Remove groups from specific drives --------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Remove-PmGroup -Path Orch1:, Orch2: TestGroup

maml:description.maml:para: Removes robot accounts from UiPath Platform Management.

The Remove-PmRobotAccount cmdlet removes robot accounts from UiPath Platform Management. Robot accounts are service accounts used for unattended automation scenarios. This cmdlet permanently removes the specified robot accounts and their associated configurations.
The -Path parameter specifies target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath Orchestrator drives (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: DELETE /api/RobotAccount/{tenantId}/{robotAccountId}
OAuth required scopes: PM.RobotAccount
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Platform Management robot account delete permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.

Specifies the names of robot accounts to remove. Multiple names can be specified as an array. This parameter is mandatory and accepts robot account names exactly as they appear in Platform Management.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This provides an additional safety check before removing robot accounts.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the removal operation. This is useful for verifying which robot accounts would be affected before making changes.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This provides an additional safety check before removing robot accounts.

Specifies the names of robot accounts to remove. Multiple names can be specified as an array. This parameter is mandatory and accepts robot account names exactly as they appear in Platform Management.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the removal operation. This is useful for verifying which robot accounts would be affected before making changes.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet permanently removes robot accounts and cannot be undone
- Use -WhatIf parameter to preview the removal operation before executing
- Robot accounts are used for unattended automation scenarios
- Removing a robot account will affect any automation processes that depend on it
- Platform Management cmdlets (starting with "Pm") provide cross-organizational robot account management
- Always verify robot account dependencies before removal to avoid disrupting automation workflows

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Remove multiple robot accounts ----------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Remove-PmRobotAccount RobotAccount1, RobotAccount2, RobotAccount3 -WhatIf

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes multiple robot accounts in a single operation.
Removes the robot account with an additional confirmation prompt before proceeding.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 3: Remove robot accounts from specific drives ----
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Remove-PmRobotAccount -Path Orch1:, Orch2: RobotAccount1

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes the robot account from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

maml:description.maml:para: Resolves directory entity names to their detailed information in UiPath Platform Management.

The Resolve-PmDirectoryNameBulk cmdlet resolves directory entity names (users, groups, or applications) to their detailed directory information in UiPath Platform Management. This cmdlet is useful for bulk resolution of multiple entity names to retrieve comprehensive directory details including identifiers, display names, organizational information, and source details.
This cmdlet accesses Platform Management APIs and works across all UiPath Orchestrator drives (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:).
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/Directory/BulkResolveByName/{partitionGlobalId}
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies the type of directory entity to resolve. Valid values include "User", "Group", and "Application". This parameter determines the type of directory object that will be returned.
Specifies the names of directory entities to resolve. This parameter accepts an array of names for bulk resolution operations. Names should match the directory entity names exactly.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the type of directory entity to resolve. Valid values include "User", "Group", and "Application". This parameter determines the type of directory object that will be returned.
Specifies the names of directory entities to resolve. This parameter accepts an array of names for bulk resolution operations. Names should match the directory entity names exactly.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Platform Management APIs work across all Orchestrator drives.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet is designed for bulk resolution operations to improve performance when resolving multiple entities
- The source property indicates the directory source (e.g., "local", "aad" for Azure Active Directory)
- Entity types must be specified exactly as "User", "Group", or "Application"
- Names must match exactly with directory entity names for successful resolution
- Platform Management cmdlets (starting with "Pm") provide cross-organizational directory information
- Use this cmdlet when you need to convert entity names to their full directory representations with identifiers

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Resolve multiple users by name ----------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Resolve-PmDirectoryNameBulk User user1@example.com, user2@example.com

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resolves multiple user names in a single operation to retrieve their directory information.

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 2: Resolve multiple groups --------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Resolve-PmDirectoryNameBulk Group Administrators, Users, Everyone

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resolves multiple group names to retrieve their directory information.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 3: Resolve and examine structure -----------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Resolve-PmDirectoryNameBulk User user@example.com | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resolves a user name and displays the complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 4: Resolve across multiple drives ----------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Resolve-PmDirectoryNameBulk -Path Orch1:, Orch2: User user@example.com

dev:remarks.maml:para: Resolves user names across multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

maml:description.maml:para: Searches Directory.

The `Search-OrchDirectory` cmdlet searches for directory users and groups in UiPath Orchestrator's directory service. This cmdlet provides a way to find users and groups by searching for names that start with the specified search term within the Orchestrator's directory context.

The search results include directory objects such as users and robot accounts, along with their identifiers, display names, domains, and type information. This cmdlet specifically searches within the Orchestrator's directory service scope, which may include different results compared to Platform Management directory searches.

The search is performed using a "starts with" pattern and includes domain context (typically "autogen" for local users), making it suitable for finding directory objects within the Orchestrator's authentication and user management context.
This cmdlet is useful for user discovery, administrative tasks, and integration scenarios where you need to find and identify directory objects within the Orchestrator environment.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/DirectoryService/SearchForUsersAndGroups?domain=autogen&prefix={prefix}&searchContext=All
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: Users.View or Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: (Users.View or Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit)
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.

maml:description.maml:para: Searches directory users and groups in Platform Management.

The `Search-PmDirectory` cmdlet searches for directory users and groups in UiPath Platform Management based on a name pattern. This cmdlet provides a way to find users, groups, and robot users across the directory by searching for names that start with the specified search term.

The search results include comprehensive information about directory objects such as users (DirectoryUser), groups (DirectoryGroup), and robot users (DirectoryRobotUser), along with their identifiers, display names, email addresses, and source information.

This is a Platform Management API cmdlet that works on any UiPathOrch drive (Orch1:, Orch1Tm:, Orch1Du:) and provides essential functionality for user and group discovery in automation workflows and administrative tasks.
The search is performed using a "starts with" pattern, making it efficient for finding directory objects when you know the beginning of the name or identifier.
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Directory/Search/{partitionGlobalId}?startsWith={userName}
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Platform Management directory search scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Directory search permissions]

Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input. Since this is a Platform Management API cmdlet, it works on any UiPathOrch drive.
Specifies the name pattern to search for. The search finds directory objects whose names start with this value. This parameter is required and supports searching for users, groups, and robot users.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name pattern to search for. The search finds directory objects whose names start with this value. This parameter is required and supports searching for users, groups, and robot users.

Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input. Since this is a Platform Management API cmdlet, it works on any UiPathOrch drive.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet uses Platform Management API and works on any UiPathOrch drive
- The search uses a "starts with" pattern rather than exact matching
- Object types include DirectoryUser (individual users), DirectoryGroup (groups), and DirectoryRobotUser (robot accounts)
- The type property contains numeric values corresponding to the object types
- The source property indicates the origin of the directory object (typically "local")
- Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete object structure and understand all available properties
- This cmdlet is useful for user/group discovery in automation scripts and administrative tasks

dev:remarks.maml:para: Searches for all directory objects (users, groups, robot users) whose names start with "y".
Searches for directory objects with the exact name or starting with "user@example.com".

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Search-PmDirectory admin | Where-Object objectType -eq "DirectoryGroup"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Searches for directory objects starting with "admin" and filters to show only groups.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $result = Search-PmDirectory user@example.com PS Orch1:\> $result | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Searches for directory objects and displays the complete structure in JSON format to explore all properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Search-PmDirectory y | Select-Object identityName, displayName, objectType

dev:remarks.maml:para: Searches for directory objects starting with "y" and displays key properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the current location to the UiPathOrch module's installation directory.

The Set-OrchLocation cmdlet changes the current location (working directory) to the directory where the UiPathOrch module is installed. This allows for quick navigation to the module's files and resources.
Primary Endpoint: (none)
OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)
Specifies the name of the module. When specified, moves to the directory where that module is installed.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.
Specifies the name of the module. When specified, moves to the directory where that module is installed.
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet. The default value is Continue.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet is a convenience function that changes your current PowerShell location. It does not return any output (Void return type).
The target directory is determined by the ModuleBase property of the loaded UiPathOrch module. If the module is not loaded, the cmdlet may fail.
You can use Push-Location before calling this cmdlet to save your current location, then use Pop-Location to return to your original location after exploring the module directory.
The module directory typically contains: - Docs: Documentation files
- Examples: Example scripts and usage sample
- Functions: PowerShell function definitions
- Localized help files (en-US, ja-JP, etc.)
- Module manifest and script files
This cmdlet is particularly useful during module development, troubleshooting, or when you need to access module resources directly.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-OrchLocation PS C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\Modules\UiPathOrch>

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the current location to the UiPathOrch module installation directory. The prompt shows the new location after execution.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Push-Location; Set-OrchLocation; Get-ChildItem PS C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\Modules\UiPathOrch> Pop-Location

dev:remarks.maml:para: Uses Push-Location to save the current location, navigates to the UiPathOrch module directory, lists the contents, and then returns to the original location using Pop-Location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-OrchLocation PS C:\Program Files\PowerShell\7\Modules\UiPathOrch> Get-ChildItem Docs

dev:remarks.maml:para: Navigates to the UiPathOrch module directory and then explores the Docs subdirectory to access documentation files.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates or updates robot accounts in organizations.

Updates the groups to which the robot account in the organization belongs. If a non-existent robot account name is specified, a new robot account will be created with that name and added to the specified group.

Multiple robot account names can be specified, separated by commas. However, please note that wildcards cannot be used in the account name. This is a trade-off for this cmdlet's ability to both create and update. For the group names, you can specify multiple comma-separated texts containing wildcards.

You can also create or update a robot account by importing a CSV file. To generate a CSV file in an importable format, run `Get-PmRobot -ExportCsv`. To see how to import this CSV file, refer to Example 2.
The column names in the CSV file correspond to each parameter name. Please refer to the help for each parameter.
Primary Endpoint:
OAuth required scopes:
Required permissions:
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill UserName Description }}

Specifies the name of the group(s) to which the specified robot account belongs. The specified robot account will be removed from any group that is not specified here. To specify GroupName in a CSV file, name the columns GroupName0, GroupName1, ..., with one group name in each. You can use up to GroupName9.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the groups for the robot account `RobotAccountName` to `MyGroup` and `YourGroup`. If a robot account with the name `RobotAccountName` does not exist, a new robot account will be created with this name and added to `MyGroup` and `YourGroup`.

Imports a CSV file to create or update robot accounts in the organization. To generate a CSV file in an importable format, run `Get-PmRobot -ExportCsv`. Please note that the Path column specified in the CSV file takes precedence over the current drive.

Any column in the CSV file can be overridden by specifying the parameter on the command line in the PS console. For example, to add a robot account to the tenant in the current drive instead of the drive specified in the Path column of the CSV file, specify `-Path .`. Alternatively, you can simply import a CSV file with the Path column removed to add/update robot accounts in the tenant of the current drive.

maml:description.maml:para: Starts UiPath robot jobs for specified processes.

Initiates job execution for specified UiPath processes in Orchestrator folders. This cmdlet creates job instances that will be executed by available robots according to the folder's robot assignments and process configurations.
Jobs are created with Pending status and will be picked up by available robots that have access to the target folder and meet the process requirements.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Jobs/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.StartJobs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs
Required permissions: Jobs.Create Processes.View
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Specifies the names of processes to start jobs for. Supports wildcards and multiple values.
Specifies the runtime type for job execution. Valid values include: Unattended, NonProduction.
Specifies the number of job instances to create for each process. Default is 1.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Prompts for confirmation before starting jobs. Recommended when starting multiple jobs.

maml:description.maml:para: Stops running or pending UiPath robot jobs.
Stops running or pending UiPath robot jobs in Orchestrator folders. This cmdlet can gracefully stop jobs or force termination when jobs are unresponsive.
Jobs in Running state will be gracefully stopped, allowing them to complete their current activity and perform cleanup. Jobs in Pending state will be cancelled before execution begins.
Use the -Force parameter to forcefully terminate unresponsive jobs, though this may result in incomplete cleanup or data inconsistency.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Jobs/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.StopJobs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs
Required permissions: Jobs.Edit
Specifies target folders. Use comma-separated values for multiple folders. Supports wildcards. If not specified, targets the current folder.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Specifies the IDs of jobs to stop. Supports multiple values.
Forces termination of jobs without graceful shutdown. Use with caution as this may result in incomplete cleanup.
Controls how progress information is displayed during cmdlet execution.
Prompts for confirmation before stopping jobs. Recommended when stopping multiple jobs.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually stopping jobs. Recommended for safety verification.
Specifies the depth of folder recursion. A depth of 0 targets only the current folder.
Forces termination of jobs without graceful shutdown. Use with caution as this may result in incomplete cleanup.

maml:alert.maml:para: Job entities are folder-scoped. You must navigate to a folder or use -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters to specify target folders.
Jobs in Running state will be gracefully stopped unless -Force is specified. Jobs in Pending state will be cancelled immediately.
Use -WhatIf to preview job termination before actual execution, especially when using pipeline input from Get-OrchJob.
Use -Force only when jobs are unresponsive and cannot be stopped gracefully, as forced termination may result in incomplete cleanup or data inconsistency.
Use -Confirm when stopping multiple jobs to review each job termination individually.

maml:description.maml:para: Stops running or pending test set executions in UiPath Orchestrator.

The Stop-OrchTestSetExecution cmdlet stops running or pending test set executions in UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet allows you to cancel test set executions that are currently running or queued for execution, which is useful for managing test automation resources and stopping tests that are no longer needed.

This is a folder entity operation. You must either navigate to a specific folder using Set-Location (cd) or use the -Path parameter to specify target folders. The -Path parameter specifies target folder paths. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - Test set execution stop API endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Test execution management scope]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Test set execution stop permissions]

Specifies the folder paths where the test set executions are located. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target test executions in specific folders.
Specifies the IDs of the test set executions to be stopped. You can specify multiple IDs to stop multiple test executions simultaneously.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when stopping test executions as it may impact ongoing testing activities.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which test set executions would be stopped before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is especially important when stopping test executions as it may impact ongoing testing activities.
Specifies the IDs of the test set executions to be stopped. You can specify multiple IDs to stop multiple test executions simultaneously.

Specifies the folder paths where the test set executions are located. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target test executions in specific folders.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which test set executions would be stopped before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target test executions in the desired location.
Stopping test set executions will immediately terminate all running test cases within those test sets. This action cannot be undone, so use caution when stopping executions.
Before stopping test executions, consider using Get-OrchTestSetExecution to identify which executions are currently running and their status.
Use the -WhatIf parameter to preview the operation, especially when using patterns that might match multiple test executions.
When stopping multiple test executions, the operation is performed sequentially. Large numbers of executions may take time to process.
For Test Manager operations, ensure you are connected to the appropriate Test Manager drive (Orch1Tm:, Orch2Tm:, etc.) or use the -Path parameter to specify the correct drive.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates the password for the current user in UiPath Orchestrator.

The Update-OrchCurrentUserURPassword cmdlet updates the password for the current user in UiPath Orchestrator. When executed without parameters, the cmdlet prompts the user to enter a new password and confirmation interactively. This cmdlet is useful for users who need to change their Orchestrator authentication password.
The cmdlet includes built-in confirmation prompts to ensure the new password is entered correctly.
This is a user-specific operation that affects only the currently authenticated user's credentials.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - User password update endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - User profile modification scope]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - User profile edit permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows updating the password across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This adds an additional confirmation step beyond the password entry prompts.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the password update operation.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows updating the password across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This adds an additional confirmation step beyond the password entry prompts.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually executing the password update operation.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet prompts interactively for password input when executed without parameters
- The password prompts include:
- Password: (for the new password) - Confirmation: (to confirm the new password) - Both password entries are masked with asterisks (*) for security
- The cmdlet requires mandatory password and confirmation parameters when executed
- Use -WhatIf to preview the operation without making changes
- This operation affects only the currently authenticated user's password
- Ensure you have appropriate network connectivity to the Orchestrator instance when changing passwords

maml:description.maml:para: Updates the version of processes.
Updates the process package referenced by the specified process to the specified version. If no version is specified, it will be updated to the latest version.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Id parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
